lions mane fruiting body.notes: Lion's mane fruiting body (Hericium erinaceus). Contains hericenones (lipophilic, fruiting body-specific) and beta-glucans. No published PK for hericenones. Beta-glucans interact with gut immune receptors rather than being absorbed systemically. Fruiting body provides different bioactive profile than mycelium (which contains erinacines). Clinical cognitive benefits demonstrated in RCTs but mechanism poorly understood.
aliases: Lions Mane Mushroom Fruiting Body Extract
Lion's Mane Mushroom Fruiting Body Extract

absorption_structured.notes: No published PK for hericenones; beta-glucans are gut-active, not systemically absorbed

lions mane standardized extract.absorption: poor (~15% for erinacines in rats; polysaccharides not absorbed intact)
lions mane standardized extract.notes: Standardized lion's mane extract (typically to polysaccharides/beta-glucans, or hericenones from fruiting body). The only published bioavailability data: erinacine S had 15.13% oral F in rats (PMID 31022946). No human PK exists. Beta-glucans are not absorbed intact — they interact with gut immune receptors (GALT/Peyer's patches) and are fermented. The nootropic compounds (hericenones, erinacines) have limited and poorly characterized PK.

absorption_structured.notes: Erinacine S 15% oral F in rats (PMID 31022946); no human PK; polysaccharides not systemically absorbed

lion's mane (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

bacosides 45%.absorption: low (~10-25% estimated; same poor solubility as 50% standardization)
bacosides 45%.notes: Standard bacopa extract standardized to 45% bacosides. Same PK limitations as BaCognize: no published bioavailability data, water-insoluble bacosides, likely gut microbiome-mediated metabolism. Clinical benefits in memory/cognition RCTs at 300 mg/day but mechanism not fully characterized. Less concentrated than BaCognize 50%.

absorption_structured.notes: Estimated; no published PK; poor solubility limits absorption

bacosides 50% (bacognize).absorption: low-moderate (~15-30% estimated; water-insoluble bacosides, no published PK)
bacosides 50% (bacognize).notes: BaCognize standardized to 50% bacosides (Natural Remedies, India). No published pharmacokinetic data exists for Bacopa or its saponins (PMC12158153, 2025 review). Bacoside A is water-insoluble with low oral bioavailability (PMC9436272). Phytosome formulations improve Cmax by 17-18% vs uncomplexed bacosides. Despite poor PK, clinical RCTs show cognitive benefits at 300-600 mg/day, suggesting gut-mediated or sufficient-at-high-dose effects.

absorption_structured.notes: No published PK data; estimated from poor solubility profile and phytosome enhancement data

bacopa (unspecified).notes: Generic Bacopa herb extract without stated bacoside standardization; conservative scoring.

bacopa.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

reishi fruiting body.notes: Ganoderma lucidum/lingzhi fruiting body. Contains triterpenes (ganoderic acids — bitter compounds) and polysaccharides (beta-glucans). Triterpenes are lipid-soluble; polysaccharides require hot water extraction. Dual-extracted products (ethanol + water) capture both fractions. Traditional 'mushroom of immortality.'

reishi standardized extract.notes: Ganoderma lucidum extract standardized to polysaccharides (typically >30%) and/or triterpenes. Dual extraction (hot water + ethanol) captures both bioactive fractions. Clinical evidence for immune modulation, sleep quality, and adaptogenic stress support. Host Defense and Real Mushrooms are quality brands.

reishi (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

reishi.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

maitake fruiting body.absorption: poor for polysaccharides (GI-local immune interaction; beta-glucans not absorbed systemically)
maitake fruiting body.notes: Whole fruiting body powder; contains beta-glucans and D-fraction polysaccharides supporting immune function.
aliases: maitake (grifola frondosa) fruiting body proprietary extract

absorption_structured.notes: Beta-glucans interact with GALT/Peyer's patches locally; not absorbed intact systemically

maitake d-fraction.absorption: poor for polysaccharides; D-fraction is a purified beta-glucan extract
maitake d-fraction.notes: Patented maitake beta-glucan extract (Maitake Products Inc). D-fraction is a protein-bound polysaccharide (~30% protein, ~70% polysaccharide). Beta-glucans are NOT absorbed systemically — they interact with gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT), dendritic cells, and macrophages via Dectin-1 receptor. Clinical studies show immune modulation effects. Nanba et al. research. Score reflects purified form quality, not systemic bioavailability.

absorption_structured.notes: Beta-glucan polysaccharide; acts via GI immune receptors, not systemic absorption

maitake (unspecified).notes: Grifola frondosa mushroom (maitake); contains beta-glucans (D-fraction, MD-fraction) with immunomodulatory properties. Preclinical evidence for blood glucose regulation. Form not specified; conservative scoring applied.

shiitake fruiting body.notes: Whole fruiting body; contains lentinan (beta-1,3-glucan), eritadenine, and B vitamins.

absorption_structured.notes: Lentinan (beta-glucan) acts locally on gut immune system; not absorbed intact

shiitake extract.notes: Standardized extract; concentrated lentinan and AHCC (Active Hexose Correlated Compound) fractions for enhanced bioavailability.

absorption_structured.notes: Active compounds (lentinan, AHCC) are polysaccharides acting via GI immune receptors

shiitake (unspecified).notes: Lentinula edodes mushroom (shiitake); source of lentinan (beta-1,3-glucan) with documented immunostimulatory activity. Contains eritadenine for cholesterol reduction. Form not specified; conservative scoring applied.

turkey tail fruiting body.notes: Whole fruiting body; contains PSK (polysaccharide-K/Krestin) and PSP polysaccharopeptides.
aliases: Turkey Tail Mushroom fruiting body extract

absorption_structured.notes: PSK/PSP polysaccharides act via gut immune system; not systemically absorbed

turkey tail standardized extract.notes: Standardized extract of Trametes versicolor, typically to polysaccharopeptides (PSK/PSP). PSK (Krestin) and PSP are protein-bound polysaccharides used as cancer adjuvant therapy in Japan. They modulate immune function via Toll-like receptors and complement activation in gut-associated lymphoid tissue. Not absorbed intact systemically. Strong clinical evidence base in oncology (meta-analyses show survival benefit as adjuvant).

absorption_structured.notes: PSK/PSP are large polysaccharopeptides; immune activity via gut receptors, not systemic absorption

turkey tail (unspecified).notes: Trametes versicolor mushroom (turkey tail); source of PSK (polysaccharide-K) and PSP with clinical evidence in oncology adjuvant therapy. Japanese approval for cancer immunotherapy support. Form not specified.

turkey_tail.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

chaga sclerotium.notes: Whole sclerotium (conk); contains betulinic acid, melanin, beta-glucans. Moderately bioavailable.

absorption_structured.notes: Polysaccharides/betulinic acid; betulinic acid has poor aqueous solubility and low oral F

chaga extract.notes: Concentrated extract; enriched in inotodiol, triterpenoids, and beta-glucans for enhanced bioavailability.

absorption_structured.notes: Hot water/ethanol extract; beta-glucans GI-local; betulinic acid has poor oral bioavailability

chaga (unspecified).notes: Inonotus obliquus mushroom (chaga); rich in betulinic acid, melanin, and polysaccharides. Traditional Siberian medicine for immune and GI support. Limited human clinical data; most evidence preclinical. Form not specified.

chaga.cui_note: No UMLS CUI established for Inonotus obliquus as a supplement ingredient

cordyceps militaris.notes: Cordyceps militaris fruiting body (cultivated). Contains cordycepin (3'-deoxyadenosine), adenosine, and polysaccharides. Cordycepin is well-absorbed but rapidly deaminated by adenosine deaminase (short t1/2). Polysaccharide beta-glucans act via gut immune receptors. Clinical studies support exercise performance, fatigue reduction, and immune modulation at 1-3 g/day. CS-4 strain is most studied.

absorption_structured.notes: Cordycepin well-absorbed but rapidly metabolized by adenosine deaminase; polysaccharides GI-local

cordyceps sinensis mycelium.notes: CS-4 strain mycelium (Paecilomyces hepiali) grown on grain substrate. NOT true wild Cordyceps sinensis (which costs $20,000+/kg). Contains cordycepin and polysaccharides but at lower concentration than C. militaris fruiting body. Traditional use for fatigue and kidney yang support. Verify mycelium vs fruiting body on label.

cordyceps (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

cordycepsprime extract.notes: Enhanced cordyceps bioavailability formula with improved absorption technology. Branded cordyceps extract with superior cellular uptake.

gelatinized maca.absorption: good (~60-75%; gelatinization removes starch, concentrating bioactives and improving digestibility)
gelatinized maca.notes: Pre-gelatinized maca root (starch removed by heat processing). Improved digestibility and bioactive concentration vs raw powder. Contains macamides, macaenes, and glucosinolates. Clinical RCTs support sexual function, energy, and mood at 1.5-3 g/day. The Peruvian maca species (Lepidium meyenii/peruvianum) has the strongest evidence base. Gelatinization does NOT remove active compounds.

absorption_structured.notes: Gelatinization improves digestibility ~30%; macamides are small lipophilic molecules with reasonable absorption

maca root powder.notes: Raw root powder; full spectrum; may cause digestive upset in sensitive individuals

maca (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

maca.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

mct oil c8/c10.notes: Medium-chain triglycerides; primarily C8 (caprylic) and C10 (capric) acids; rapid energy source

c8 mct oil (pure caprylic).notes: Pure caprylic acid (C8:0) MCT oil — the fastest-converting MCT for ketone production. Bypasses normal fat digestion — directly absorbed via portal vein to liver for rapid beta-oxidation. Brain Octane (Bulletproof) is the best-known C8 brand. 5-15 mL/day. Start low to avoid GI distress. Most efficient MCT for ketosis.

c10 mct oil (capric acid).notes: Capric acid (C10:0) MCT oil. Slightly slower ketone conversion than C8 but still rapidly absorbed via portal vein. Some evidence for unique antimicrobial properties. Often found blended with C8 in MCT oil products. Ketone production is ~50% of C8 efficiency per gram but may have broader metabolic effects.

mct_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

cla 80%.notes: Generic CLA supplement standardized to 80% conjugated linoleic acid isomers (c9,t11 + t10,c12). Semi-synthetic (isomerized from safflower linoleic acid). Evidence for modest fat loss (~0.05 kg/week, meta-analysis) and lean mass preservation. Less studied than branded Clarinol/Tonalin forms.

clarinol.notes: Patented CLA form; clinically studied; 80% CLA with optimal c9,t11 and t10,c12 ratio

tonalin.notes: BASF Tonalin CLA from safflower oil; 80% active isomers (c9,t11 + t10,c12 in 1:1 ratio). Clinically validated for body fat reduction at 3.2-6.4 g/day. Semi-synthetic (alkaline isomerization). The original branded CLA — larger evidence base than Clarinol. GMP-manufactured.

psyllium husk powder.notes: Gold-standard gel-forming soluble fiber (Metamucil). NOT systemically absorbed — acts entirely within GI lumen. Forms viscous gel that increases stool bulk, binds bile acids, and slows glucose absorption. FDA-approved health claim for cholesterol reduction (7+ g/day) and glycemic control. Most clinically studied fiber supplement with robust evidence for IBS, constipation, and cardiovascular risk reduction (PMC10389520). Bio_score reflects form quality/efficacy, not systemic absorption.

absorption_structured.notes: Not systemically absorbed; acts as gel-forming soluble fiber in GI lumen. Absorption metric not applicable.

psyllium seed.notes: Whole Plantago ovata seeds. Contains both soluble (husk) and insoluble fiber components. Less gel-forming capacity than husk alone — husk is the primary active fraction. Used for bowel regularity at 5-10 g/day. FDA-approved health claim for cholesterol when consumed as part of low-fat diet.

psyllium (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

psyllium.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

beta-glucan.notes: Beta-1,3/1,6-glucan from yeast cell wall (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) or oat/mushroom sources. Immune modulator via Dectin-1 receptor on macrophages and dendritic cells. Wellmune is the most clinically studied brand (250 mg/day). FDA-approved heart health claim for oat beta-glucan at 3g/day.

beta_glucan.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

lactobacillus acidophilus la-14.notes: IFF/HOWARU strain in 50+ studies and 18 clinical trials. RCTs (paired with HN001) show reduced BV and VVC recurrence in women and restored healthy vaginal Nugent scores. Broad immune-modulating properties.

lactobacillus acidophilus ncfm.notes: Patented strain; extensively researched; supports immune function and gut barrier

lactobacillus acidophilus dds-1.notes: Novonesis (formerly UAS Labs) strain, isolated 1959 by Prof. Khem Shahani at Univ. of Nebraska. RCT in 330 IBS adults showed significant abdominal pain improvement vs placebo (PMID:32019158). RCT demonstrated lactose intolerance symptom relief (PMID:27773241). RCT in 96 preschool children with atopic dermatitis showed improved SCORAD scores and immune markers. Proven acid/bile resistance and gut persistence with confirmed colonization in human persistence study.

lactobacillus acidophilus (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

lactobacillus acidophilus la-5.notes: Highly clinically validated strain with 150+ publications, 60+ clinical trials. Often combined with BB-12. Chr. Hansen's flagship L. acidophilus strain.

absorption_structured.notes: Chr. Hansen strain; good acid tolerance

lactobacillus acidophilus la-02.notes: L. acidophilus strain from Probiotical. Part of multi-strain formulations for digestive health.

absorption_structured.notes: Probiotical SpA strain; moderate GI survival

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Lactobacillus acidophilus | 2571 (Number of products:1845)

bifidobacterium lactis hn019.notes: IFF/HOWARU strain with 180+ publications. RCTs show reduced gut transit time and improved bowel frequency in constipation. Also enhances NK cell activity in aging adults.

bifidobacterium lactis bl-04.notes: IFF/HOWARU strain. RCT in 310 active adults reduced upper respiratory tract illness risk by 27%. Rhinovirus challenge study showed reduced viral load and primed IL-8 immune response. Often combined with LA-14 or Bi-07.

bifidobacterium lactis bi-07.notes: Clinically studied strain with RCTs for immune support and upper respiratory health. DuPont HOWARU strain.

bifidobacterium lactis (unspecified).notes: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis — widely studied probiotic species. Specific strain identification yields higher scoring.

bifidobacterium lactis bb-12.notes: Most documented Bifidobacterium strain with 400+ scientific publications. Chr. Hansen flagship strain for digestive health, immune support, and infant health.

bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis.notes: General entry for B. animalis subsp. lactis strains. Includes well-known strains like BB-12, HN019, Bi-07. Robust strain with good acid and bile tolerance.

absorption_structured.notes: Robust acid/bile tolerance for a Bifidobacterium

bifidobacterium_lactis.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient
bifidobacterium_lactis.cui_note: Corrected from the bifidum concept to the accepted Bifidobacterium animalis / lactis lineage concept.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Bifidobacterium animalis lactis | 2576 (Number of products:999)

lactobacillus rhamnosus gg.notes: Most researched probiotic strain; supports digestive health, immune function, and skin health

lactobacillus rhamnosus hn001.notes: Highly researched strain with RCTs for gestational diabetes, eczema prevention (to age 11), mental well-being, and immune function. Branded as HOWARU Rhamnosus and DR20.

lactobacillus rhamnosus (unspecified).notes: Lactobacillus rhamnosus — one of the most researched probiotic species. Specific strain (GG, HN001) yields higher scoring.

lactobacillus rhamnosus lr-04.notes: L. rhamnosus strain from Probiotical. Used in multi-strain formulations for immune and digestive support.

lactobacillus rhamnosus gr-1.notes: Most researched probiotic for women's urogenital health with multiple RCTs for UTI and BV prevention. Often combined with RC-14. Chr. Hansen strain.

absorption_structured.notes: Urogenital health strain; survives GI transit

lactobacillus rhamnosus crl-1505.notes: Immunobiotic strain studied for respiratory immunity via mucosal IgA stimulation. GRAS. Smaller clinical evidence base than LGG (primarily Argentine studies and animal models) but unique immunomodulatory properties not shared by other L. rhamnosus strains.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Lactobacillus rhamnosus | 2513 (Number of products:1133)

lactobacillus plantarum lp299v.notes: Probi AB strain (DSM 9843). Among the strongest IBS evidence bases with multiple RCTs showing significant reduction in abdominal pain frequency and severity. Survives stomach acid and bile. Reduces bacterial translocation and modulates epithelial inflammation.

lactobacillus plantarum dr7.notes: Kaneka Probiotics strain isolated from Malaysian cow milk. RCT in 111 stressed adults showed reduced stress/anxiety scores, lower cortisol, and improved cognitive function. Modulates serotonin and dopamine pathways via tryptophan metabolism.

lactobacillus plantarum (unspecified).notes: Lactobacillus plantarum — versatile probiotic species with strong GI colonization. Specific strain (LP299v, DR7) yields higher scoring.

lactobacillus plantarum l-137 (heat-kill.notes: Heat-killed Lactobacillus plantarum L-137 is used as a postbiotic immune-support ingredient. It is a distinct branded/form identity from live L. plantarum probiotic strains and is tracked separately for clean label resolution.

absorption_structured.notes: Postbiotic identity tracked separately from live probiotic strains; no oral PK-style absorption metric is typically reported.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Lactobacillus plantarum | 2573 (Number of products:1020)

bifidobacterium longum bb536.notes: Morinaga Milk strain with 50+ years of research. RCTs show reduced Japanese cedar pollinosis symptoms (eye/nasal), reduced cold/flu duration in children, and constipation relief in elderly. Modulates Th1/Th2 immune balance.

bifidobacterium longum 35624.notes: Alimentary Health / Align (P&G) strain. One of the most evidence-backed strains for IBS with multiple RCTs showing reduced abdominal pain, bloating, and bowel irregularity. Adheres to intestinal epithelium, reducing gut barrier disruption.

bifidobacterium longum (unspecified).notes: A key probiotic strain that colonizes the colon, supporting immune and digestive health. Often included in comprehensive probiotic formulations.

bifidobacterium longum subsp. infantis.notes: Specifically adapted to infant gut environment, helps with digestion of breast milk oligosaccharides and supports infant immune development.

absorption_structured.notes: Good colonization in infant gut; moderate adult GI survival

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Bifidobacterium longum | 2570 (Number of products:1018)

lactobacillus casei lc-11.notes: DuPont/Danisco strain used in multi-strain formulas. Limited standalone RCT data. General L. casei class evidence supports reduction in antibiotic-associated diarrhea and gut motility improvement. Wide pH/temperature stability aids formulation.

lactobacillus casei shirota.notes: Yakult proprietary strain (reclassified L. paracasei Shirota). RCTs show 53% reduction in URTI incidence in office workers, stress-induced gut microbiota preservation, and enhanced NK cell activity. One of the most studied probiotic strains globally.

lactobacillus casei (unspecified).notes: A versatile probiotic strain that supports digestive health and immune function. Often confused with L. paracasei due to genetic similarity.

absorption_structured.notes: Moderate GI survival; Shirota strain well-studied

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Lactobacillus casei | 2572 (Number of products:796)

bifidobacterium bifidum bb-06.notes: DuPont/Danisco strain used in multi-strain formulas. RCT evidence for improved waist circumference and antioxidant enzyme activity in overweight women. Also studied for ADHD in children (12-week RCT).

bifidobacterium bifidum r0071.notes: Lallemand Rosell-71 strain. RCT in 581 stressed students showed fewer cold/flu days and reduced stress-associated diarrhea. Canadian immune health claim for reducing cold-like symptoms in stressed adults.

bifidobacterium bifidum (unspecified).notes: A beneficial probiotic strain naturally present in the human gut microbiome, supporting digestive health and immune function. Known for its ability to help maintain healthy gut barrier function.

bifidobacterium bifidum bb-01.notes: B. bifidum strain from Probiotical. Limited strain-specific clinical evidence; general B. bifidum benefits for gut barrier and immune support.

bifidobacterium_bifidum.cui_note: Corrected from the breve concept to the accepted Bifidobacterium bifidum concept.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Bifidobacterium bifidum | 2574 (Number of products:1057)

saccharomyces boulardii cncm i-745.notes: Beneficial yeast probiotic; clinically proven for diarrhea prevention and C. diff support

saccharomyces boulardii (unspecified).notes: Probiotic yeast with inherent acid resistance (viable pH 2-8) and natural antibiotic resistance. Extensive clinical evidence for antibiotic-associated diarrhea and C. difficile. Does not require enteric coating for GI survival. One of the most clinically validated probiotic organisms.

absorption_structured.notes: Yeast — viable pH 2-8; natural antibiotic resistance

saccharomyces_boulardii.rxcui_note: GSRS collapses Saccharomyces boulardii to generic Saccharomyces cerevisiae / brewer's yeast; keep RxNorm null unless a reviewed exact probiotic strain identity is available.

lactobacillus paracasei lpc-37.notes: IFF/HOWARU strain. Dual RCTs (Sisu, ChillEx) demonstrate reduced perceived stress, anxiety, and physiological stress markers in healthy adults at 5 weeks. Also studied for atopic dermatitis and anti-allergy mucosal IgA stimulation.

lactobacillus paracasei (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

lactobacillus paracasei l.casei 431.notes: Clinically studied Chr. Hansen strain with RCTs for immune function, respiratory infections, and enhanced vaccine response.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|bacteria|Lactobacillus paracasei | 2699 (Number of products:511)

streptococcus thermophilus st-21.notes: DuPont/Danisco strain. Limited strain-specific RCT data. Class-level evidence supports reduced diarrhea duration, IBS symptom relief (bloating, flatulence), and skin ceramide improvement. Primarily a supporting strain in multi-strain blends.

streptococcus thermophilus (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a specific form for better bioavailability.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Bacteria|Streptococcus Thermophilus | 1975 (Number of products:453)

feverfew extract (parthenolide).notes: Standardized feverfew extract (Tanacetum parthenium) typically containing 0.2-0.7% parthenolide. Multiple RCTs show efficacy for migraine prophylaxis at 50-100mg/day. Parthenolide inhibits NF-kB and serotonin release from platelets. MIG-99 (CO2 extract) is the most studied formulation. Cochrane review (2015) supports modest benefit for migraine frequency reduction.

feverfew powder.notes: Dried feverfew leaf powder (Tanacetum parthenium). Parthenolide content varies widely (0.1-1.0%) depending on harvest time and storage. Some older RCTs used freeze-dried leaf powder. Less consistent than standardized extracts but retains full phytochemical matrix.

feverfew (unspecified).notes: Form not specified; conservative scoring applied. Consider using a standardized extract for better parthenolide consistency.

grass-fed butter powder.notes: Whole-food dairy fat powder used in keto and coffee-style supplement blends. Identity is tracked for clean label resolution rather than premium nutrient scoring; it can contribute butterfat, naturally occurring fat-soluble vitamins, and butyrate-class lipids.

wheatgrass juice powder.notes: Whole-food wheatgrass juice powder identity used in greens products. It is tracked as a distinct grass juice ingredient for label resolution; direct comparative PK data are limited and scoring is intentionally conservative.

fermented goat whey protein.notes: Goat whey protein identity used in fermented dairy-based supplement formulas. It is kept separate from cow whey and from non-whey goat milk protein concentrates to preserve label specificity.

gamma_butyrobetaine_ethyl_ester.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept; not an approved drug substance.

gbb ethyl ester hcl.absorption: prodrug; ethyl ester cleaved to release GBB, then hydroxylated to L-carnitine
gbb ethyl ester hcl.notes: Gamma-butyrobetaine ethyl ester HCl (GBB-EXT) — prodrug ester of gamma-butyrobetaine. Substrate for gamma-butyrobetaine hydroxylase (BBOX1), the final enzyme in endogenous L-carnitine biosynthesis. Used in pre-workout supplements to increase carnitine production and induce sweating (thermogenic effect). Limited human clinical data; mechanism extrapolated from carnitine biosynthesis pathway biochemistry. CAS for parent GBB: 6249-56-5, PubChem CID: 22620.

absorption_structured.notes: Prodrug mechanism; ethyl ester improves GI absorption vs parent GBB HCl. No published human PK data.

_metadata.name: Ingredient Quality Map
_metadata.description: Comprehensive quality mapping database for all active ingredients found in dietary supplements and medications. Each ingredient form is scored based on bioavailability, absorption rate, clinical evidence, and manufacturing quality.

bio_score.description: Base bioavailability/quality score for the ingredient form

guidelines.14-15: Premium forms with clinically proven superior absorption (chelates, liposomal, patented tech)
guidelines.11-13: High-quality forms with good clinical evidence
guidelines.5-7: Basic or unspecified forms, conservative scoring

natural_bonus.description: Bonus points if the form is naturally-derived (not synthetic)
criteria: Derived from food or plant sources

total_score.note: Unspecified forms receive lower bio_score (5-7) due to quality uncertainty

_metadata.pipeline_integration: Used by enrichment script to assign quality scores to matched ingredient forms. Match ingredient name against forms aliases (case-insensitive). Use highest-scoring matched form.
matching_notes: Match ingredient name + forms text against aliases array (case-insensitive)
If no specific form match, use 'unspecified' form with conservative scoring
Aliases include: brand names, abbreviations, scientific names, common variants
Enhanced aliases include 'supplement' suffix variants for better matching
clinical_sources: PubMed: Iron bisglycinate vs sulfate studies
NIH Office of Dietary Supplements fact sheets

changes: Added match_rules block to all ingredients
Added tart_cherry, spearmint, button_mushroom, auricularia parents (P1/P2)
Fixed royal_sun_blazei natural flag and added extract form
Added 18 batch alias sets for GardenOfLife unmapped ingredients
Fixed flax lignan fallback (added to lignans parent)
Added EXCLUDED_LABEL_PHRASES: 35% alcohol, ionic minerals, alkaline proteases

_metadata.cleanup_notes: Resolved within-ingredient alias collisions; removed 63 redundant unspecified forms

audit_runbook.purpose: Keep ingredient_quality_map.json CUI-clean and GSRS/UNII-safe without forcing ingredient families, blends, tissues, or source-specific extracts into one misleading identity.
workflow: 1. Run the live IQM CUI audit first and keep only VERIFIED or ANNOTATED_NULL states; any invalid or mismatched CUI must be resolved before GSRS enrichment.
2. Run verify_unii.py in IQM dry-run mode before applying. Only entries that pass the GSRS name/CAS gate should be auto-filled with UNII and gsrs metadata.
3. Treat governed-null GSRS outcomes as intentional when the ingredient is a class, pair, blend, tissue, extract family, or source-specific oil that does not map to one exact reviewed GSRS substance.
4. If a safe exact GSRS synonym exists, prefer adding that synonym to the parent aliases and rerunning the audit instead of forcing a looser botanical or constituent match.
5. After any GSRS write, rerun verify_unii.py and the targeted regression suite before treating the file as production-clean.

unii_audit_policy.goal: Populate exact FDA GSRS/UNII identity for atomic IQM ingredients while keeping broad classes, blends, tissues, and source-specific extracts out of false-precision substance joins.
unii_audit_policy.authority_boundary: UNII, GSRS regulatory/context fields, active moiety, salt/parent mappings, and opportunistic RxCUI come from GSRS. CUI remains UMLS authority; CAS and PubChem CID remain chemical-identity authority.
unii_audit_policy.governed_null_policy: Reviewed GSRS misses or broader-than-exact matches are handled through curated GSRS policies and must remain null-UNII rather than collapsing the ingredient to a constituent, marker compound, or adjacent source material.
unii_audit_policy.release_gate: Production acceptance requires zero unexpected GSRS rejects or not-found items for scorable atomic IQM ingredients. Remaining null-UNII states must be covered by curated GSRS policy or explicit multi-concept governance.

_metadata.cui_note: No UMLS CUI for supplement ingredient

silicon (standard).notes: Silicon supplement (typically as orthosilicic acid or choline-stabilized orthosilicic acid/BioSil). Essential trace mineral for collagen synthesis, bone minerite formation, and connective tissue integrity. Absorption depends heavily on form — orthosilicic acid is best absorbed. 5-10 mg/day elemental silicon.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Mineral|Silicon | 146 (Number of products:1149)

fluoride (standard).notes: Supplemental fluoride (typically as sodium fluoride). Supports dental enamel mineralization and reduces dental caries. Present in fluoridated water at ~0.7 ppm. Supplementation primarily for children in non-fluoridated water areas. Narrow therapeutic window — excess causes fluorosis.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|mineral|Fluoride | 2968 (Number of products:4)

alpha gpc (standard).notes: Alpha-glycerophosphocholine — a highly bioavailable choline source that crosses the blood-brain barrier. Precursor to acetylcholine and phosphatidylcholine. Clinical evidence for cognitive support in Alzheimer's (1200 mg/day) and growth hormone secretion in athletes (600 mg). ~40% choline by weight.

alpha_gpc.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
alpha_gpc.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

bcaa 2:1:1.notes: The most common and clinically studied ratio of Leucine, Isoleucine, and Valine. It is highly effective for muscle protein synthesis and recovery.

absorption_structured.notes: Free-form BCAAs rapidly absorbed via active amino acid transporters; ~90-95% oral F

bcaa 4:1:1.notes: A BCAA ratio with a higher concentration of Leucine to maximize muscle protein synthesis, often preferred for bodybuilders and athletes.

absorption_structured.notes: Free-form BCAAs rapidly absorbed via active amino acid transporters; ~90-95% oral F

instantized bcaas.notes: BCAAs that have been treated for enhanced solubility in water, making them easier to mix and absorb quickly. Does not alter bioavailability but improves user experience.

absorption_structured.notes: Free-form BCAAs rapidly absorbed via active amino acid transporters; ~90-95% oral F

bcaa peptides.notes: BCAAs linked into short peptide chains. This form may be absorbed even faster than free-form BCAAs, potentially improving muscle delivery.

absorption_structured.notes: Free-form BCAAs rapidly absorbed via active amino acid transporters; ~90-95% oral F

branched chain amino acids (standard).notes: Generic BCAA supplement (leucine, isoleucine, valine). Ratio and form unspecified. BCAAs are rapidly absorbed (~90-95% oral F) via active amino acid transport. Support muscle protein synthesis, exercise recovery, and fatigue reduction. Specify ratio (2:1:1 or 4:1:1) for accurate scoring.

branched_chain_amino_acids.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
branched_chain_amino_acids.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

docosapentaenoic acid dpa (standard).notes: Docosapentaenoic acid (DPA, 22:5 n-3) — an intermediate omega-3 between EPA and DHA. Found naturally in seal oil and human breast milk. Emerging evidence for anti-inflammatory, antithrombotic, and endothelial protection distinct from EPA/DHA. Limited supplemental form availability.

docosapentaenoic_acid_dpa.cui_note: Corrected from branded SUPER DPA oral solution concept to ingredient-level docosapentaenoic acid concept.
docosapentaenoic_acid_dpa.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

omega 6 fatty acids (standard).notes: Omega-6 fatty acids (primarily linoleic acid and arachidonic acid). Essential fatty acids obtained from vegetable oils, nuts, and seeds. Most Western diets are omega-6 sufficient; supplementation rarely needed. GLA (gamma-linolenic acid, from evening primrose/borage) is the primary supplemented omega-6.

omega_6_fatty_acids.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
omega_6_fatty_acids.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Fatty Acid|Omega-6 Fatty Acids | 2163 (Number of products:16)

omega 9 fatty acids (standard).notes: Omega-9 fatty acids (primarily oleic acid). Non-essential — the body can synthesize them. Abundant in olive oil, avocado, and macadamia nuts. Supplementation provides convenience for those with low dietary intake. Cardioprotective effects primarily from dietary sources rather than supplements.

omega_9_fatty_acids.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
omega_9_fatty_acids.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

omega 7 fatty acids (standard).notes: Omega-7 fatty acids (primarily palmitoleic acid, 16:1 n-7). Found in sea buckthorn oil and macadamia nuts. Emerging evidence for metabolic health — may improve insulin sensitivity and reduce inflammation. Purified palmitoleic acid supplements (Provinal) remove the pro-inflammatory palmitic acid co-contaminant.

omega_7_fatty_acids.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
omega_7_fatty_acids.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

other fatty acids (standard).notes: Miscellaneous fatty acid supplement. May include unusual or specialty fatty acids not classified under standard omega categories. Conservative scoring reflects unspecified identity. Check label for specific fatty acid content and concentration.

other_fatty_acids.cui_note: Class entry spanning multiple uncategorized fatty acids. No single UMLS concept is accurate.
other_fatty_acids.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

epa dha (standard).notes: Combined EPA+DHA omega-3 supplement. Provides both primary marine omega-3 fatty acids in a single product. Most fish oil supplements fall in this category. Combined dose of 1-4 g/day EPA+DHA recommended for cardiovascular health. AHA recommends ~1 g/day for secondary prevention.

epa_dha.cui_note: No confirmed exact UMLS concept for the combined EPA/DHA shorthand without forcing a branded product.
epa_dha.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

d beta hydroxybutyrate bhb (standard).notes: Exogenous beta-hydroxybutyrate (BHB) ketone body supplement. Available as sodium, calcium, or magnesium BHB salts, or as BHB esters (more expensive, better ketosis). Rapidly elevates blood ketone levels for energy, cognitive support, and ketogenic diet assistance. 6-12 g BHB typical dose.

d_beta_hydroxybutyrate_bhb.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient
d_beta_hydroxybutyrate_bhb.cui_note: Corrected from combination product concept to beta-hydroxybutyrate ingredient concept.

tudca (standard).notes: Tauroursodeoxycholic acid — a water-soluble bile acid conjugate. Cytoprotective via unfolded protein response modulation and anti-apoptotic pathways. Clinical evidence for liver protection (cholestasis), neuroprotection, and endoplasmic reticulum stress reduction. 250-1000 mg/day. Well-absorbed orally.

tudca.cui_note: Mapped to taurursodiol, the closest ingredient-level UMLS concept for TUDCA.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|chemical|Tauroursodeoxycholic Acid | 3564 (Number of products:2)

probiotic unspecified (standard).notes: No specific strain identified on label. Clinical benefits are strain-specific and cannot be assumed from species alone. Look for a named strain (e.g., LA-14, LP299v, HN019) and CFU count for meaningful assessment.

probiotic_unspecified.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
probiotic_unspecified.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

5-loxin.notes: Standardized to 30% AKBA, patented extract. Superior anti-inflammatory activity.
aliases: joint shield 5-loxin advanced boswellia serrata extract

boswellia aflapin.notes: Patented Boswellia serrata extract (Laila Nutra) with enhanced bioavailability via proprietary process. Contains both boswellic acids and non-boswellic acid synergists. Clinical RCTs show faster onset of joint pain relief vs standard extracts. 100 mg/day effective dose. Superior to 5-Loxin in some head-to-head comparisons.
aliases: apresflex indian frankincense boswellia serrata extract
aprèsflex indian frankincense boswellia serrata extract

boswellia standardized extract.notes: Boswellia serrata extract standardized to 65% boswellic acids (AKBA is the most active). Anti-inflammatory via 5-LOX inhibition. Multiple RCTs support efficacy for osteoarthritis and IBD at 300-500 mg/day. Generic standardized extracts are less studied than 5-Loxin or Aflapin branded forms.
aliases: boswellia boswellia serrata gum resin extract
boswellin super (boswellia serrata) gum resin extract
AKBA, Beta-Boswellic Acids, total organic and Boswellic Acid

boswellia resin powder.notes: Raw Boswellia serrata resin (frankincense) in powder form. Contains boswellic acids at lower concentration than standardized extracts. Traditional Ayurvedic use for joint inflammation. Requires higher doses (1-3 g/day) due to variable boswellic acid content. Food-matrix bound actives slow release.

boswellia (unspecified).notes: Boswellia serrata resin containing boswellic acids (AKBA). Anti-inflammatory via 5-LOX inhibition. Form not specified; conservative scoring applied.

boswellia.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

thymoquinone standardized.notes: Nigella sativa oil standardized for thymoquinone (TQ) content (typically 3-5%). TQ is the primary bioactive with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. Clinical evidence for glycemic control, lipid modulation, and respiratory support. BioPerine may enhance TQ absorption.

cold-pressed black seed oil.notes: Cold-pressed Nigella sativa seed oil preserving full spectrum of fatty acids, thymoquinone, and volatile compounds. Natural TQ content (~1-3%) lower than standardized extracts. Rich in linoleic acid and oleic acid. Traditional Islamic medicine use ('the cure for everything except death').
aliases: black cumin seed oil, unrefined, virgin
thymocid organic black cumin seed oil
thymocid black cumin organic nigella oil

black seed oil.notes: Standard Nigella sativa (black cumin) seed oil. Processing method unspecified. Contains thymoquinone, thymol, and essential fatty acids. Evidence for anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and metabolic benefits at 1-3 g/day or 200-600 mg TQ/day.

black seed powder.notes: Ground Nigella sativa seeds. Lower TQ bioavailability than oil due to matrix binding. Contains fiber, protein, and minerals in addition to thymoquinone. Traditional spice use. Requires higher doses than oil forms for equivalent TQ delivery.

black seed (unspecified).notes: Nigella sativa (black seed/black cumin). Contains thymoquinone, thymol, and essential fatty acids. Traditional Islamic medicine use. Form/processing unspecified; conservative scoring.

sea buckthorn seed oil.notes: Cold-pressed oil from Hippophae rhamnoides seeds. Rich in ALA (omega-3), linoleic acid (omega-6), and vitamin E. Unlike berry oil, seed oil does NOT contain palmitoleic acid (omega-7). Supports skin health, mucosal integrity, and cardiovascular function. Sibu and SeabuckWonders are leading brands.
aliases: sea buckthorn hippophae rhamnoides berry pulp and seed oil
Sea Buckthorn (Hippophae rhamnoides) berry pulp and seed Oil

sea buckthorn berry oil.notes: Oil from sea buckthorn fruit pulp/peel. Uniquely rich in palmitoleic acid (omega-7, 30-40%) and carotenoids (giving orange color). Supports mucosal membranes (vaginal dryness, dry eye), skin health, and cardiovascular function. Different fatty acid profile from seed oil.

sea buckthorn extract.notes: Concentrated extract from sea buckthorn berries. Contains flavonoids (isorhamnetin, quercetin), carotenoids, and vitamin C. Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory. Less studied than the oil forms but provides polyphenol benefits not present in pure oil.

sea buckthorn powder.notes: Dried sea buckthorn berry/leaf powder. Contains fiber, vitamin C, and polyphenols but lower concentration of fatty acids than oil forms. Provides broader nutrient profile including minerals. Used in smoothies and traditional preparations.

sea buckthorn (unspecified).notes: Hippophae rhamnoides berry/seed oil; rich in palmitoleic acid (omega-7), flavonoids, and vitamins C/E. Traditional Tibetan/Ayurvedic medicine. Form unspecified.

sea_buckthorn.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

primavie shilajit.notes: Patented purified shilajit extract (Natreon Inc). Standardized to >50% fulvic acid and dibenzo-α-pyrones. Clinical RCTs show testosterone support, CoQ10 enhancement, and exercise performance improvement. GMP-certified purification removes heavy metals. Most clinically studied shilajit brand.

purified shilajit extract.notes: Shilajit processed to remove heavy metals and contaminants while preserving fulvic acid and dibenzo-α-pyrones. Purification is critical — raw shilajit from Himalayan/Altai sources may contain unsafe levels of lead, arsenic, or mycotoxins. Look for third-party heavy metal testing.

fulvic acid.notes: Primary bioactive component of shilajit. Fulvic acid is a complex mixture of humic substances that enhances mitochondrial function and mineral transport. May improve CoQ10 bioavailability and support cellular energy production. Also found in soil/compost independently of shilajit.

shilajit resin.notes: Semi-purified shilajit in tar-like resin form. Traditional Ayurvedic preparation (mineral pitch). Contains fulvic acid, trace minerals, and dibenzo-α-pyrones. Less processed than extracts — verify heavy metal testing. Traditional dose 300-500 mg/day dissolved in warm water.

shilajit powder.notes: Dried powdered shilajit. May be less concentrated than resin or extract forms. Easier to encapsulate. Verify purification and heavy metal testing — raw shilajit can contain dangerous contaminant levels. Lower fulvic acid concentration than purified extracts.

shilajit (unspecified).notes: Mineral pitch (mumijo) from Himalayan rock exudate; contains fulvic acid, dibenzo-alpha-pyrones, and trace minerals. Ayurvedic adaptogen. Form unspecified; conservative scoring.

shilajit.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

testofen.notes: Patented fenugreek extract (Gencor) standardized to 50% Fenuside (furostanol saponins). Multiple RCTs show testosterone support, libido improvement, and body composition benefits in men at 600 mg/day. FDA GRAS. The most clinically studied fenugreek extract for male health.

fenugreek standardized extract.notes: Fenugreek (Trigonella foenum-graecum) seed extract standardized to saponins or 4-hydroxyisoleucine. Clinical evidence for glycemic control (insulin sensitization), lactation support, and testosterone modulation. 500-1000 mg/day typical dose. Multiple branded extracts (Testofen, Fenulife).

fenugreek seed powder.notes: Ground whole fenugreek seeds. Contains galactomannan fiber, saponins, and 4-hydroxyisoleucine at lower concentrations than extracts. Traditional use as spice and galactagogue. Higher doses needed (2-5 g/day) vs extracts. Fiber content may support glycemic control independently.

fenugreek (unspecified).notes: Trigonella foenum-graecum seed containing furostanol saponins (protodioscin), galactomannan fiber, and 4-hydroxyisoleucine. Supports glycemic control and testosterone. Form unspecified.

testosurge extract.notes: Branded fenugreek extract with enhanced saponins standardization. Clinically studied for testosterone support and male health.

fenugreek.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

remifemin.notes: Patented black cohosh extract (Schaper & Brümmer, Germany). The most clinically studied botanical for menopausal symptoms — over 100 clinical studies. Standardized to triterpene glycosides (including actein and 27-deoxyactein). 40 mg/day is the standard dose. European Medicines Agency approved for menopausal hot flashes.

black cohosh standardized extract.notes: Cimicifuga racemosa (Actaea racemosa) root/rhizome extract standardized to 2.5% triterpene glycosides. Clinical evidence supports hot flash reduction and menopausal symptom relief. Mechanism debated: may act via serotonergic pathways rather than phytoestrogen activity. Rare hepatotoxicity reports; monitor liver function.

black cohosh root powder.notes: Ground black cohosh root/rhizome. Lower triterpene concentration than standardized extracts. Traditional Native American use for women's health. Variable potency between batches due to lack of standardization. Higher doses needed. Same hepatotoxicity precaution as extracts.

black cohosh (unspecified).notes: Actaea racemosa (Cimicifuga) rhizome; contains triterpene glycosides (actein, 23-epi-26-deoxyactein). Evidence for menopausal vasomotor symptoms. Form unspecified; conservative scoring.
aliases: black cohosh (cimicifuga racemosa) root and rhizome extract
black cohosh (actaea racemosa) root & rhizome extract
black cohosh root and rhizome extract
cimicifuga racemosa root and rhizome extract

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Black Cohosh | 1068 (Number of products:476)

st john's wort standardized extract.notes: Hypericum perforatum extract standardized to 0.3% hypericin and/or 3-5% hyperforin. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to SSRIs for mild-moderate depression (Cochrane Review 2008). CRITICAL drug interactions: induces CYP3A4 and P-gp, reducing efficacy of oral contraceptives, warfarin, cyclosporine, and many other drugs. 300 mg TID standard dose.
aliases: st. john's wort extract standardized to 0.3% hypericin

st john's wort powder.notes: Ground Hypericum perforatum aerial parts (flowering tops). Lower hypericin/hyperforin concentration than standardized extracts. Traditional use for mood support. Variable potency. Same CYP3A4 induction concern — avoid concurrent medications metabolized by CYP3A4.

st john's wort (unspecified).notes: Hypericum perforatum herb; contains hypericin and hyperforin. Well-documented antidepressant for mild-moderate depression (Cochrane review). CYP3A4/P-gp inducer. Form unspecified.
aliases: st. john's wort whole herb extract
st. john's wort flower, leaf extract
st. john's wort flower head extract
st. john's wort aerial parts extract

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|St. John's Wort | 1034 (Number of products:354)

valerian standardized extract.notes: Valeriana officinalis root extract standardized to valerenic acids (typically 0.8%). Clinical evidence for mild sleep improvement and anxiety reduction, though results are mixed across meta-analyses. May modulate GABA-A receptors. Generally safe with minimal hangover effect. 300-600 mg before bedtime standard dose.

valerian root powder.notes: Ground Valeriana officinalis root. Contains valerenic acids, valepotriates, and volatile oils at lower concentrations than standardized extracts. Traditional European sedative herb. Characteristic strong odor. Higher doses needed (2-3 g) vs extracts for sleep support.

valerian (unspecified).notes: Valeriana officinalis root; contains valerenic acid and valepotriates. GABA-A receptor modulator for sleep and anxiolysis. European Pharmacopoeia monograph. Form unspecified; conservative scoring.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Valerian | 131 (Number of products:679)

vitex standardized extract.notes: Vitex agnus-castus berry extract (typically standardized to 0.5% agnuside or 6% aucubin). Clinical evidence for PMS symptom relief, luteal phase support, and cycle regulation. Acts on dopamine D2 receptors to reduce prolactin. 20-40 mg/day standard dose. Clinically studied brands include Femicur and Mastodynon.

chasteberry powder.notes: Ground Vitex agnus-castus berries. Traditional European herb for women's cycle regulation and PMS. Lower agnuside/aucubin concentration than standardized extracts. Variable potency. May take 2-3 cycles for full effect. Contraindicated with dopamine agonists and hormone therapies.

chasteberry (unspecified).notes: Vitex agnus-castus berry; contains diterpenes (rotundifuran) and iridoids (agnuside, aucubin). Dopaminergic action reduces prolactin. Evidence for PMS and menstrual irregularities. Form unspecified.

chasteberry.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

bergamot bpf.notes: Bergamot Polyphenolic Fraction — patented extract from Citrus bergamia (Calabria, Italy). Standardized to naringin, neoeriocitrin, and neohesperidin. Clinical RCTs show significant LDL-C and triglyceride reduction at 500-1000 mg/day. H&AD (Herbal & Antioxidant Derivatives) is the patent holder. May improve endothelial function.

bergamot standardized extract.notes: Citrus bergamia extract standardized for polyphenol content. Contains statin-like HMG-CoA reductase inhibiting flavonoids. Less specifically standardized than BPF but still provides bergamot polyphenols for cardiovascular/lipid support.

bergamot (unspecified).notes: Citrus bergamia extract; contains statin-like HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors (brutieridin, melitidin). Evidence for LDL/triglyceride reduction. Form unspecified; conservative scoring.

citrus_bergamot.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

guava leaf extract.notes: Psidium guajava leaf extract rich in quercetin, catechin, and gallic acid. Clinical evidence for postprandial glucose reduction (inhibits alpha-glucosidase) and antidiarrheal activity. Japanese functional food ingredient (Bansoureicha). 400-1000 mg/day for glycemic support.

guava leaf powder.notes: Ground Psidium guajava leaves. Traditional tropical medicine for diarrhea, diabetes, and wound healing. Contains polyphenols and tannins. Lower bioactive concentration than standardized extracts. Used as tea preparation traditionally.

guava (unspecified).notes: Psidium guajava leaf extract; contains quercetin glycosides and gallic acid. Traditional anti-diarrheal and antidiabetic use. Form unspecified; conservative scoring.

algal triglyceride.notes: DHA from microalgae (Schizochytrium or Crypthecodinium) in triglyceride form. Bioequivalent to fish oil TG — GMR 112% (96-130%) in 93-adult RCT (PMID 41096614). Vegan/vegetarian source. Free of ocean contaminants (mercury, PCBs). Life's DHA (DSM) is the leading brand. Environmental advantage over fish-derived DHA.

DHA fish oil ethyl ester.notes: DHA as ethyl ester — chemically modified form with lower bioavailability than TG or rTG (~27% less than natural TG, Dyerberg 2010). Synthetic ester bond requires cleavage before absorption. Found in prescription omega-3 concentrates (Lovaza). Less efficient than triglyceride forms.

DHA fish oil triglyceride.notes: DHA in natural fish oil triglyceride form. Standard bioavailability reference. Well-absorbed with fat-containing meals. Most standard DHA supplements use this form unless otherwise specified on label.

DHA krill phospholipid.notes: DHA bound to phospholipids from Antarctic krill (Euphausia superba). Enhanced acute absorption vs fish oil TG in some studies. Contains astaxanthin naturally. Phospholipid-bound DHA may preferentially incorporate into cell membranes. Superba Krill is a major branded source.

dha (unspecified).notes: DHA form not specified. Conservative scoring. DHA (docosahexaenoic acid, 22:6 n-3) is critical for brain, retina, and cardiovascular health. Form significantly affects absorption — check product label.

DHA fish oil rTG.notes: DHA concentrated via re-esterified triglyceride processing. High bioavailability (~24% above natural TG). Enzymatic process selectively concentrates DHA content while maintaining natural triglyceride backbone for efficient lipase digestion and absorption.

dha.notes: Omega-3 fatty acid essential for brain and eye health. Algal forms are vegan and highly bioavailable.
dha.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|fatty acid|Docosahexaenoic Acid | 4096 (Number of products:5)

EPA fish oil ethyl ester.notes: EPA as ethyl ester — lower bioavailability than TG forms (~27% less). Vascepa (icosapent ethyl) is a prescription EPA-EE product for cardiovascular risk reduction. Synthetic ester requires lipase cleavage before absorption. Take with fat-containing meal.

EPA fish oil triglyceride.notes: EPA in natural fish oil triglyceride form. Standard bioavailability. Well-absorbed with dietary fat. Most standard EPA supplements unless label specifies otherwise. EPA is the direct precursor to anti-inflammatory resolvins and protectins.

EPA krill phospholipid.notes: EPA bound to phospholipids from Antarctic krill. Enhanced bioavailability vs standard fish oil. EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid, 20:5 n-3) is the primary anti-inflammatory omega-3. Phospholipid form may improve cell membrane incorporation. Contains astaxanthin naturally.

epa (unspecified).notes: EPA form not specified. Conservative scoring. EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid) is the primary anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acid. Form affects bioavailability — check product label.

EPA fish oil rTG.notes: EPA in re-esterified triglyceride form. ~24% higher bioavailability than natural TG (Dyerberg 2010). Enzymatic concentration process. EPA-dominant rTG products target inflammation, cardiovascular risk, and mood support at 1-4 g/day.

epa.notes: Omega-3 fatty acid with anti-inflammatory properties. Important for cardiovascular and mental health.
epa.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Fatty Acid|EPA (Eicosapentaenoic Acid) | 1011 (Number of products:2641)

phytoceramides (plant).notes: Plant-derived ceramides (typically from rice, wheat, or sweet potato). Ceramides are sphingolipids critical for skin barrier function. Oral phytoceramides at 350 mg/day showed improved skin hydration in RCTs. Lipowheat and Ceramosides are branded sources. Natural plant origin.

wheat-derived.notes: Ceramides extracted from wheat oil (Lipowheat). FDA GRAS-affirmed. Clinical studies show skin hydration improvement after 4 weeks at 350 mg/day. Contains glucosylceramides that are metabolized to ceramides in the gut. Wheat allergen concern for celiac/gluten-sensitive individuals.

synthetic.notes: Chemically synthesized ceramides. Identical molecular structure to natural ceramides but produced via chemical synthesis rather than plant extraction. May have batch-to-batch consistency advantages. Less studied in oral supplement context than plant-derived forms.

ceramides (unspecified).notes: Ceramide form not specified. Conservative scoring. Ceramides are essential skin barrier lipids. Oral supplementation targets skin hydration and anti-aging. Check label for plant vs synthetic source.

ceramides.notes: Skin lipids that support moisture barrier. Phytoceramides from plants are well-absorbed.
ceramides.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
ceramides.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

citrus complex.notes: Mixed bioflavonoids from citrus peel (hesperidin, naringin, rutin, quercetin). Support capillary integrity and vitamin C recycling. Typically standardized to total bioflavonoid content. Often combined with vitamin C supplements to enhance antioxidant activity and absorption.

hesperidin-rich.notes: Bioflavonoid complex standardized for high hesperidin content from citrus peel. Hesperidin is the most abundant citrus flavonoid. Supports venous tone, capillary permeability, and anti-inflammatory pathways. Diosmin-hesperidin combinations used clinically for chronic venous insufficiency.

rutin complex.notes: Bioflavonoid complex rich in rutin (quercetin-3-rutinoside) from buckwheat, citrus, or eucalyptus. Rutin supports capillary strength, anti-inflammatory activity, and antioxidant defense. Metabolized by gut bacteria to quercetin. Historically used for vascular fragility and bruising.

bioflavonoids (unspecified).notes: Bioflavonoid form not specified. Conservative scoring. Bioflavonoids are a diverse class of plant polyphenols that support antioxidant defense and vascular health. Check label for specific composition.

bioflavonoids.notes: Plant polyphenols that support vitamin C activity and vascular health.
bioflavonoids.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
bioflavonoids.rxcui_note: Generic bioflavonoids entry spans multiple flavonoid families and plant sources; keep RxNorm null unless the record is split to a specific source such as citrus bioflavonoids.

hydrolyzed silk protein.notes: Enzymatically hydrolyzed silk fibroin from Bombyx mori (silkworm cocoon). Rich in serine (~33%), glycine (~44%), and alanine (~29%). Peptides are more bioavailable than intact silk protein. Used for skin, hair, and joint support. Traditionally consumed in Asian medicine.

silk peptides.notes: Low-molecular-weight peptides (typically <3 kDa) from silk fibroin hydrolysis. Better absorption than intact protein due to small peptide size. May support collagen synthesis and skin hydration. Limited clinical evidence for oral supplementation vs topical use.

silk amino acids (unspecified).notes: Silk amino acid form not specified. Conservative scoring. Derived from silkworm cocoon protein. Rich in serine, glycine, and alanine. Check label for hydrolyzed or peptide specification.

silk_amino_acids.notes: Protein derived from silk with amino acid profile supporting skin and hair health.
silk_amino_acids.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

grass-fed desiccated.notes: Freeze-dried organ meat from grass-fed, pasture-raised cattle (typically liver, heart, kidney). Grass-fed sourcing provides higher levels of fat-soluble vitamins (A, K2), CoQ10, and B12 vs grain-fed. Ancestral Supplements and Heart & Soil are major brands. Concentrated micronutrient source.

freeze-dried.notes: Organs preserved via lyophilization (freeze-drying), maintaining heat-sensitive nutrients (enzymes, vitamins). Superior nutrient retention vs conventional desiccation. Typically sourced from bovine liver, heart, kidney, or multi-organ blends. Popular in nose-to-tail nutrition approach.

standard desiccated.notes: Conventionally dried organ meat powder. Heat processing may reduce some heat-sensitive nutrients (enzymes, certain B-vitamins) compared to freeze-dried. Still provides heme iron, B12, copper, and organ-specific peptides. More affordable than freeze-dried or grass-fed options.

organ extracts (unspecified).notes: Organ extract form not specified. Conservative scoring. Desiccated organ supplements provide concentrated nutrients from animal organs (liver, heart, kidney). Check label for source animal, organ type, and processing method.

organ_extracts.notes: Nutrient-dense organ meat extracts. Grass-fed sources preferred for quality.
organ_extracts.cui_note: Class entry spanning multiple glandular and organ extracts. No single UMLS concept is accurate.
organ_extracts.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

aliases: concentrated European Hemp (Cannabis sativa) stalk and seed Oil

cold-pressed organic.notes: Organic hemp seed oil pressed without heat (≤49°C). Preserves full spectrum of omega-3 (ALA), omega-6 (GLA, LA), and phytosterols. Ideal 3:1 omega-6:omega-3 ratio. USDA organic certification ensures no pesticides or GMO. Contains no CBD/THC. Nutiva and Manitoba Harvest are leading brands.

cold-pressed.notes: Hemp seed oil pressed without heat to preserve polyunsaturated fatty acids. Rich in ALA (omega-3), linoleic acid (omega-6), and gamma-linolenic acid (GLA). Balanced omega profile for cardiovascular and skin health. Non-organic but still cold-pressed for nutrient preservation.

refined.notes: Heat-processed or solvent-extracted hemp seed oil. Refining removes some phytonutrients, chlorophyll, and volatile compounds. Neutral taste and longer shelf life vs cold-pressed. Lower GLA and phytosterol content. Primarily a cooking/carrier oil rather than supplement-grade.

hemp seed oil (unspecified).notes: Hemp seed oil form not specified. Conservative scoring. Hemp seed oil provides balanced omega-3/6/9 fatty acids and GLA. Contains no significant CBD or THC. Check label for cold-pressed specification.

hemp_seed_oil.notes: Rich in omega-6 GLA and omega-3 ALA. Not to be confused with CBD oil.
hemp_seed_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|fat|Hemp oil | 2812 (Number of products:90)

calamari oil phospholipid.notes: Calamari-derived omega-3 in phospholipid form. Enhanced bioavailability and cell membrane incorporation vs TG. Contains naturally occurring DHA-rich phospholipids. Less commonly available than TG form. Premium pricing reflects phospholipid extraction process.

calamari oil triglyceride.notes: Omega-3 oil from deep-sea squid (Dosidicus gigas or Illex) in natural triglyceride form. Naturally high in DHA (up to 50% of total omega-3). Sustainable marine source with lower heavy metal burden than large predatory fish. Well-absorbed via standard lipase digestion pathway.

calamari oil (unspecified).notes: Calamari oil form not specified. Conservative scoring. Squid-derived omega-3 naturally high in DHA. Check label for triglyceride or phospholipid specification.

calamari_oil.notes: Sustainable omega-3 source very high in DHA. Good alternative to fish oil.
calamari_oil.cui_note: Mapped to squid oil, the closest ingredient-level UMLS concept for calamari oil.
calamari_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

tuna oil molecular distilled.notes: Tuna oil purified via molecular distillation to remove mercury, PCBs, and other contaminants. Critical for tuna-sourced oils due to bioaccumulation in large predatory fish. Purification does not change the molecular form (remains TG). Score reflects purity improvement.

tuna oil triglyceride.notes: Omega-3 oil from tuna (typically Thunnus species) in natural triglyceride form. Higher DHA:EPA ratio than most fish oils. Standard lipase-dependent absorption. Mercury content concern with tuna — molecular distillation recommended for purification.

tuna oil (unspecified).notes: Tuna oil form not specified. Conservative scoring. Tuna oil provides DHA-rich omega-3. Mercury contamination is a concern — verify third-party testing or molecular distillation.

tuna_oil.notes: Fish oil from tuna, high in DHA. Check for mercury testing/purification.
tuna_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
tuna_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

fermented.notes: Traditionally fermented cod liver oil (Green Pasture is the primary manufacturer). Lacto-fermentation process preserves natural vitamin A, D3, and omega-3 without heat. Contains fat-soluble vitamins in natural ratios. Controversial — some third-party tests have questioned rancidity markers. Premium pricing.

virgin/cold-processed.notes: Cod liver oil extracted without heat or chemical solvents. Preserves natural vitamins A and D3 alongside EPA/DHA. Rosita Extra Virgin Cod Liver Oil is the best-known brand. Minimal processing maintains full spectrum of cofactors. Take with fat for optimal absorption.

cod liver oil molecular distilled.notes: Cod liver oil purified via molecular distillation. Removes contaminants (mercury, PCBs) but also strips natural vitamins A and D, which must be re-added synthetically. Carlson's and Nordic Naturals use this process. Trade-off: purity vs loss of naturally-occurring cofactors.

cod liver oil (unspecified).notes: Cod liver oil form not specified. Conservative scoring. Cod liver oil uniquely provides omega-3 + vitamins A and D. Form affects vitamin retention — check label for processing method.
aliases: highly purified norwegian cod liver oil

cod_liver_oil.notes: Traditional omega-3 source also containing vitamins A and D. Fermented is highest quality.
cod_liver_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Fatty Acid|Cod Liver Oil | 390 (Number of products:43)

umf 15+ / mgo 514+.notes: Medical-grade manuka honey (Leptospermum scoparium, NZ). UMF 15+ corresponds to MGO 514+ mg/kg — strong antibacterial activity. Methylglyoxal (MGO) is the primary bioactive. Clinical evidence for wound healing, H. pylori inhibition, and upper respiratory support. Comvita and Manuka Health are leading brands. Expensive; verify UMF certification authenticity.

umf 10+ / mgo 263+.notes: Mid-grade manuka honey. UMF 10+ / MGO 263+ provides meaningful antibacterial activity above regular honey. Suitable for general health maintenance and mild throat/digestive support. More affordable than UMF 15+. Still requires verified New Zealand UMF trademark certification.

umf 5+ / mgo 83+.notes: Entry-level manuka honey. UMF 5+ / MGO 83+ has mild antibacterial activity above regular honey but below therapeutic grades. Suitable as a premium honey for general wellness use. Limited clinical evidence at this MGO concentration for specific health claims.

ungraded manuka.notes: Manuka honey without official UMF or MGO grading. May contain low or variable methylglyoxal levels. Without certified testing, antibacterial activity cannot be verified. Price should reflect uncertainty. Consider purchasing UMF-certified products for health purposes.

manuka honey (unspecified).notes: Manuka honey grade not specified. Conservative scoring. Manuka's benefits depend on MGO content — verify UMF or MGO certification on label.

manuka_honey.notes: New Zealand honey with methylglyoxal (MGO) antibacterial activity. UMF/MGO rating indicates potency. Higher ratings = more therapeutic.
manuka_honey.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound
manuka_honey.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

aliases: highly refined and concentrated omega-3 fish oil
concentrated fish oil omega-3 ethyl esters
red sockeye salmon oil natural wild

triglyceride (rTG) form.notes: Re-esterified triglyceride — the gold-standard supplemental fish oil form. ~24% higher bioavailability than natural TG (Dyerberg et al. 2010, PMID 20638827). Enzymatic re-esterification concentrates EPA/DHA while restoring the natural triglyceride backbone. Nordic Naturals Ultimate Omega is a well-known rTG product. Only 55-60% of typical 'rTG' products are truly re-esterified (PMC9882700).

natural triglyceride.notes: Fish oil in its natural triglyceride form — the baseline reference for bioavailability comparisons. Well-absorbed (~70-80% of EPA+DHA reaches systemic circulation). Requires pancreatic lipase for digestion. Take with a fat-containing meal for optimal absorption. Most standard fish oil softgels contain this form.
aliases: ultra pure high potency fish oil
ultra pure, high potency fish oil
msc coc mercury-free tuna fish oil
puresea omega3 mercury free tuna oil

ethyl ester.notes: Chemically modified fish oil where fatty acids are bound to ethanol instead of glycerol. ~27% lower bioavailability than natural TG (Dyerberg 2010, PMID 20638827). Requires pancreatic lipase cleavage before absorption. Lovaza/Omacor (prescription) uses this form. Cheaper to manufacture but less efficiently absorbed than TG or rTG forms.

molecularly distilled.notes: Fish oil purified via molecular distillation to remove heavy metals, PCBs, and dioxins. The distillation process itself does not change the molecular form (typically remains TG). Considered a purification method rather than a distinct bioavailability class. Score reflects purity benefit minus minimal processing impact.

fish liver oil.notes: Fish liver oil contains EPA/DHA plus vitamins A and D. Cod liver oil is the most common form.

fish oil phospholipid.notes: Omega-3 bound to phospholipids, primarily from krill oil (Euphausia superba). ~33% higher AUC than standard fish oil TG in single-dose crossover studies (Schuchardt et al. 2011, PMC3168413). Contains astaxanthin antioxidant naturally. Neptune Krill Oil and Superba are leading brands. May cause fewer fishy burps due to phospholipid emulsification.

fish oil (unspecified).notes: Fish oil form not specified on label. Conservative scoring applied. Could be TG, EE, or rTG — form significantly affects bioavailability (up to 70% difference between worst and best). Check product label for specific form information.
aliases: red sockeye salmon oil, natural, wild

fish_oil.notes: Source of EPA/DHA omega-3s. Triglyceride forms absorb better than ethyl esters. Look for IFOS certified.
fish_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Fatty Acid|Fish Oil | 68 (Number of products:1588)

propolis extract.notes: Bee propolis extract — resinous antimicrobial mixture bees produce from tree buds and sap. Brazilian green propolis (BGP) is rich in artepillin C (prenylated cinnamic acid, up to 30% dry weight); European/Chinese types are CAPE/flavonoid-dominant. Human PK (PMID 33688872) shows artepillin C circulates ~89% as glucuronide conjugates with <5% free compound. Preclinical evidence for anticancer, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activity across both types.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Propolis | 1666 (Number of products:205)

royal jelly.notes: Honeybee pharyngeal gland secretion. Contains 10-HDA (trans-10-hydroxy-2-decenoic acid), royalactin, and acetylcholine. Clinical evidence for cholesterol reduction, blood sugar modulation, and skin health. Highly perishable — freeze-dried forms preferred. Allergen risk for bee-product-sensitive individuals.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Animal Part or Source|Royal Jelly | 589 (Number of products:264)

chia seed.notes: Salvia hispanica seeds. Rich in ALA omega-3 (~60% of fat), soluble/insoluble fiber (34%), and protein (19%). Forms mucilaginous gel in water — slows glucose absorption and provides satiety. ALA conversion to EPA/DHA is limited (~5% EPA). Antioxidant content extends shelf life.
aliases: raw cold pressed chia seed oil

papaya fruit.notes: Carica papaya fruit extract. Contains papain (proteolytic enzyme), chymopapain, and carotenoids (lycopene, beta-cryptoxanthin). Papain supports protein digestion (acts in GI tract, not absorbed). Fermented papaya extract (FPP/Immun'Age) has clinical evidence for antioxidant and immune support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Papaya | 99 (Number of products:619)

flaxseed oil.notes: Cold-pressed Linum usitatissimum seed oil. Richest plant source of ALA (alpha-linolenic acid, ~50-60% of fatty acids). ALA conversion to EPA/DHA is very limited (~5% EPA, <0.5% DHA). Supports cardiovascular health via ALA-specific pathways. Lignan content depends on processing.
aliases: lignans within the flax seed oil
organic cold pressed flax seed oil
100% pure cold-pressed organic flax seed oil
cold pressed omega rich flax seed oil
vegan cold-pressed omega-rich flax seed oil

flaxseed meal/powder.notes: Whole/ground flaxseed preparations provide ALA and lignans but are not equivalent to purified oil forms.

flaxseed.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

chamomile extract.notes: Matricaria chamomilla (German chamomile) extract. Contains apigenin (GABA-A receptor agonist), bisabolol, and chamazulene. Multiple RCTs support mild anxiolytic and sleep-promoting effects. Also used for GI comfort and mild anti-inflammatory action. Generally safe; avoid with blood thinners.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Chamomile (unspecified) | 2257 (Number of products:206)

lemon balm extract.notes: Melissa officinalis leaf extract. Contains rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and volatile oils. Clinical evidence for anxiety reduction, cognitive support, and mild sedation via GABA-transaminase inhibition. Cyracos is a clinically studied branded extract. 300-600 mg/day typical dose.

lemon_balm.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

olive leaf extract standardized.notes: Olea europaea leaf extract standardized to oleuropein (typically 18-25%). Oleuropein and hydroxytyrosol provide antioxidant, antimicrobial, and cardiovascular benefits. EFSA-approved health claim for olive polyphenol protection of LDL from oxidation. 500-1000 mg/day typical dose.

olive leaf extract.notes: Olea europaea leaf extract containing oleuropein, hydroxytyrosol, and other polyphenols. Antioxidant, antimicrobial, and cardiovascular support. Less precisely standardized than oleuropein-standardized versions. Traditional Mediterranean remedy for infections and hypertension.

hydroxytyrosol (olive phenolic).notes: Olive phenolic with EU health claim (EFSA 2011): >=5 mg/day protects LDL from oxidative damage — one of the strongest evidence-backed EU Article 13.1 supplement claims. RCT (n=40, PMID 19007325): 25 mg/day x 3 wk reduced LDL oxidation markers. Cardioprotective via SIRT1 activation, NF-kB suppression, direct radical scavenging. Rapidly absorbed: Tmax ~30 min, urinary recovery 35-55% as metabolites.

absorption_structured.notes: Urinary recovery 35-55% as metabolites in human crossover; Tmax ~30 min; t1/2 ~1.5h (PMID 19007325, 21443285)

tyrosol (olive phenolic).notes: Simple phenylethanol in olive oil (0.9-6 mg/100g EVOO). Oral bioavailability ~75-90% with rapid plasma appearance (PMID 11754880). Oral bioavailability 43.3% as total salicylates (PMID 11599656); salicin hydrolyzed to salicylic acid within 2h. Effective at 120-240 mg/day for low back pain and OA in RCTs (PMID 25997859). Broader mechanism than aspirin: NF-kB/TNF-a downregulation + flavonoid synergy (PMID 21226125). Less GI risk than aspirin.

Positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors — anxiolytic without diazepam-like sedation (PMID 24893163). Anti-inflammatory via COX-2/NF-kB suppression. Anti-tumor activity via STAT3 inhibition (PMID 23394783). Human RCT data limited to small anxiolytic pilots. Often co-standardized with magnolol (5:1 to 10:1 honokiol:magnolol ratios).

D-phenylalanine's pain-modulating mechanism is theoretical with limited clinical evidence.

Traditional Southeast Asian adaptogenic tonic.

Absolute oral F ~10.5% in rat (PMID 16206032); standardized water extract matrix improves relative bioavailability (PMC6161288). Human RCTs used whole-root extract, not isolated eurycomanone.

Human PK (PMC7994819) found intact asiaticoside F = 1.86%; asiatic acid accumulates with repeated dosing. Clinical evidence for wound healing, venous insufficiency, and cognitive support. Phase 1 trial (PMID 35204098) confirmed 250-500 mg doses safe.

Only PA-certified products should be considered safe. Short-term use (up to 12 weeks) is rated possibly safe. Contraindicated for pregnant/breastfeeding women and those with liver disease.

Cochrane review (PMID 24395557): supports short-term LBP relief, effect comparable to 12.5 mg rofecoxib. RCT (n=197, PMID 16083521): WS 1531 extract (50 mg harpagoside/day) reduced chronic LBP VAS by -25 mm at 12 wk vs placebo. COX-1/COX-2 inhibition at therapeutic levels.

calcium pyruvate (unspecified).notes: Calcium salt of pyruvic acid, a key glycolysis/TCA cycle intermediate. Dissociates in GI tract; pyruvate absorbed ~70% via monocarboxylate transporters. Weight loss RCTs (PMID 1542005, PMID 8290828) found modest fat loss at extreme doses (22-44 g/day) — standard 2-5 g supplement doses are far below studied doses. No meaningful ergogenic evidence at supplement-level dosing.

humic acid (unspecified).notes: Humic acid is a heterogeneous mixture of high-MW polycarboxylic acids from decomposed plant/microbial matter in soil and peat. Oral bioavailability is essentially unstudied — polymeric structure (MW 10,000-100,000 Da) makes systemic absorption unlikely. In vitro antiviral activity reported (PMID 27137840) but no human RCTs. Safety data insufficient; FDA considers GRAS only from food-grade sources.

cobalt (unspecified).notes: Cobalt is essential solely as the central atom of vitamin B12 (cobalamin). Inorganic cobalt supplementation does NOT substitute for B12 deficiency. Absorbed 10-40% via DMT1 iron transporters. Excess is nephrotoxic and cardiotoxic; causes polycythemia at high doses (WADA banned). No established human RDA separate from B12. Supplemental inorganic cobalt provides no documented benefit.

bile extract (unspecified).notes: Bile extract (ox bile) contains conjugated bile acids (taurocholic/glycocholic), phospholipids, cholesterol. Amphipathic molecules emulsify dietary fats enabling pancreatic lipase access and fat-soluble vitamin absorption (A, D, E, K). In enterohepatic cycle 90-95% reabsorbed in terminal ileum. Supports fat digestion post-cholecystectomy. Doses 100-500 mg/meal.
