apoaequorin (unspecified).notes: Calcium-binding photoprotein from Aequorea victoria jellyfish, marketed as Prevagen. Produced via recombinant E. coli fermentation. Subject to complete proteolytic digestion in GI tract — probability of intact apoaequorin reaching CNS is near zero. FDA warning letter (2012) and FTC deceptive advertising complaint (2017). Published RCT (PMID 26869340) criticized for post-hoc subgroup analysis.

arjuna (unspecified).notes: Terminalia arjuna bark extract, an Ayurvedic cardiovascular tonic. Contains triterpenoids (arjunolic acid, arjunic acid) and polyphenols. Triterpenoid oral bioavailability estimated <10% by analogy to oleanolic acid. Preclinical evidence for cardioprotection, lipid-lowering, and antioxidant effects. threonic acid (unspecified).notes: Threonic acid is an endogenous oxidative breakdown product of L-ascorbic acid (vitamin C). Typically represents the threonate ligand in magnesium L-threonate (Magtein), which facilitates Mg transport across the BBB, elevating CSF Mg2+ by 7-15% vs other Mg salts (Liu et al., Neuron 2010). Open-label dementia trial used Mg-threonate (PMID 32162987). As isolated ingredient, no independent bioactivity demonstrated.

centrophenoxine (unspecified).notes: Centrophenoxine (meclofenoxate, Lucidril) is a synthetic ester of DMAE and p-chlorophenoxyacetic acid. Oral hydrolysis releases DMAE → choline → acetylcholine. pCPA moiety enhances DMAE BBB penetration. Clinical evidence weak and inconsistent; most trials underpowered (ALZDF: insufficient evidence). Classified as unapproved drug in dietary supplements by FDA (PMID 35959800). DMAE trial (n=242 AD) reported serious AEs including fatal cardiac failure.

absorption_structured.notes: Moderate oral absorption (~55%); hydrolyzed to DMAE + pCPA; DMAE crosses BBB

tart_cherry.description: Montmorency tart cherry (Prunus cerasus). Rich in anthocyanins, flavonols, and melatonin precursors. Clinical evidence supports exercise-induced muscle recovery, sleep quality improvement, and inflammation modulation. Low absolute anthocyanin bioavailability (~0.6%) offset by dose optimization in trials.

tart_cherry.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

vitacherry sports extract.notes: VitaCherry® Sports by FutureCeuticals. Montmorency tart cherry, 15x anthocyanin concentration vs standard powders. Multiple RCTs: 8/9 studies reduced exercise-induced muscle soreness; 4/4 studies improved sleep quality (PMID 33440334, 31986108, 40964149). Low absolute bioavailability (~0.6% anthocyanins) offset by concentrated dose. Dose: 200-500mg extract/day.

absorption_structured.notes: ~0.6% peak anthocyanin bioavailability; plasma Cmax 1-2h post-dose. Clinically effective due to high dose concentration despite low absolute absorption.

tart cherry extract.notes: Standard Montmorency tart cherry extract. Good clinical evidence for muscle recovery and sleep. Lower anthocyanin concentration than standardized forms. Dose: 60-90mL concentrate or 300-500mg extract/day.
aliases: cherrypure tart cherry fruit & skin powder

absorption_structured.notes: Anthocyanin bioavailability ~0.6-2%; formulation quality significantly affects absorption.

tart cherry (unspecified).notes: Prunus cerasus (Montmorency cherry) fruit; rich in anthocyanins (cyanidin-3-glucosylrutinoside) and melatonin. Evidence for sleep quality, post-exercise recovery, and uric acid reduction. Form unspecified.

spearmint.description: Spearmint (Mentha spicata). Rich in rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and phenolic compounds. Standard extracts used for digestion and flavoring. Proprietary high-polyphenol variants (Neumentix) clinically studied for cognitive performance and focus.
spearmint.cui_note: No UMLS CUI for supplement ingredient

neumentix spearmint extract.notes: Neumentix® by Kemin Industries. Proprietary high-polyphenol spearmint bred for elevated rosmarinic acid and 50+ phenolic compounds. 5 clinical trials support cognitive performance, reaction time, and working memory at 900mg/day x 90 days; onset at 7 days (PMID 29314866, 30802720). Superior to standard spearmint extract.

absorption_structured.notes: Polyphenol bioavailability assumed good based on water-soluble phenolic compounds; no direct pharmacokinetic data available.

spearmint extract.notes: Standard spearmint extract. Polyphenol content variable between suppliers. Limited clinical evidence for cognitive effects; primarily used as digestive aid and flavoring.

absorption_structured.notes: Rosmarinic acid and flavonoids have moderate polyphenol bioavailability.

spearmint (unspecified).notes: Mentha spicata herb; contains rosmarinic acid, carvone, and limonene. Evidence for PCOS anti-androgen activity (PMID 19585478) and cognitive enhancement (Neumentix). Form unspecified.

button_mushroom.description: White button mushroom (Agaricus bisporus). Rich in beta-glucans, ergothioneine, glutathione, and polysaccharides. Supports immune function via Dectin-1 receptor activation. Antioxidant and prebiotic effects. Primarily culinary; more modest medicinal evidence than reishi or maitake.

button mushroom fruiting body extract.notes: Extract from Agaricus bisporus fruiting bodies. Beta-glucans activate macrophages and NK cells. button mushroom (unspecified).notes: Agaricus bisporus (white/cremini mushroom); contains ergothioneine, beta-glucans, and conjugated linoleic acid. Only natural food source of ergothioneine. Aromatase-inhibiting properties. Form unspecified.

auricularia (unspecified).notes: Auricularia auricula-judae (wood ear/black fungus) mushroom; contains polysaccharides with anticoagulant and hypoglycemic properties. Traditional Chinese medicine for blood stasis. Form unspecified.

andrographis.description: Andrographis paniculata (King of Bitters). Active compound andrographolide has immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Clinical evidence supports reduction in severity and duration of upper respiratory tract infections. Proprietary standardized forms (AP-Bio, ParActin) show 4–6× higher andrographolide bioavailability via lipid-matrix delivery.
andrographis.cui_note: No UMLS CUI for supplement ingredient
andrographis.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

ap-bio andrographis extract.notes: AP-Bio™ by HP Ingredients. Standardized Andrographis paniculata leaf extract with enhanced bioavailability lipid matrix. ParActin® (Natural Alternatives International) is the same andrographolide-standardized extract class. Multiple clinical trials: reduced cold duration, immune activation (PMID 19657410, 11103571). Dose: 200–400mg/day standardized to ≥10% andrographolide.

absorption_structured.notes: Lipid-matrix delivery enhances andrographolide absorption vs standard powder. Base andrographolide oral bioavailability only ~2-5% (PMID 21721007); AP-Bio lipid matrix may improve this 4-6x.

andrographis extract.notes: Standard Andrographis paniculata extract. Typically standardized to 10% andrographolide. Good clinical evidence for immune support and reduction of upper respiratory symptoms. Dose: 400–1200mg/day.

absorption_structured.notes: Andrographolide absolute oral bioavailability ~2.67% in rats (PMID 21721007); limited by gut-bacterial sulfonate conversion and P-gp efflux. Human Cmax ~393 ng/mL at Tmax 1.5-2h (PMID 11081986)

andrographis (unspecified).notes: Generic andrographis listing without standardization specified. Clinical RCTs use extracts standardized to 10% andrographolide at 400-1200 mg/day for immune support and upper respiratory symptoms.

sage.description: Salvia officinalis (Common Sage). Rich in rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and polyphenols. Well-documented cholinesterase inhibitory activity and antioxidant properties. Multiple RCTs support cognitive performance enhancement in healthy adults, particularly memory, attention, and processing speed (PMID 16205659, 20377818). Cognivia® (Nexira) is a proprietary high-polyphenol extract with clinical backing.
sage.cui_note: No UMLS CUI for supplement ingredient

cognivia sage extract.notes: Cognivia® by Nexira. Proprietary Salvia officinalis leaf extract standardized to high ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid. Multiple RCTs: improved cognitive performance, working memory, and attention in healthy adults (PMID 16205659, 20377818). Dose: 300–600mg/day. Superior polyphenol profile vs standard sage.

absorption_structured.notes: Rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid bioavailability moderate-good; phenolic matrix enhances uptake.

sage extract.notes: Standard Salvia officinalis extract. Polyphenol content variable. Clinical evidence for cognitive and memory support. Dose: 300–600mg/day leaf extract.

absorption_structured.notes: Polyphenol bioavailability moderate; rosmarinic acid well-absorbed.

sage (unspecified).notes: Salvia officinalis herb; contains rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and thujone. Evidence for cognitive enhancement, menopausal hot flashes, and sore throat. German Commission E approved. Form unspecified.

kefir_culture.description: Traditional fermented kefir starter culture containing a symbiotic consortium of lactic acid bacteria, acetic acid bacteria, and yeasts embedded in kefir grains. Provides diverse probiotic strains plus beta-glucan polysaccharides from kefir grain matrix. Evidence supports gut microbiome diversity, immune modulation, and lactose digestion improvement.

kefir culture (fermented).notes: Traditional fermented kefir grain culture (Eastern European origin). Consortium of Lactobacillus, Leuconostoc, Acetobacter species plus Saccharomyces and Kluyveromyces yeasts. Provides diverse probiotic strains; evidence for gut health, immune function, and lactose digestion (general fermented dairy evidence). Dose: varies by CFU content.

absorption_structured.notes: Live culture viability and colonization depends on CFU count, acid tolerance, and gut transit conditions.

kefir (unspecified).notes: Symbiotic culture of bacteria and yeast (SCOBY) producing fermented milk. Contains Lactobacillus kefiranofaciens, L. kefiri, and kefiran polysaccharide. Evidence for gut health and bone density. Form unspecified.

phenylethylamine (unspecified).notes: Beta-phenylethylamine (PEA) is an endogenous trace amine from L-phenylalanine, found in fermented foods and chocolate. Oral bioavailability severely limited: >90% first-pass MAO-B deamination (t1/2 ~5-10 min oral; PMC3904499, PMID 24482732). Acts via TAAR1 receptor inducing dopamine/norepinephrine efflux (PMID 15447669, PMID 18182557). Clinically relevant only with concurrent MAO-B inhibitor. Supplement mood/focus effect likely minimal without MAOI.

absorption_structured.notes: Severely limited by MAO-B first-pass deamination (>90%); t1/2 ~5-10 min oral; functional bioavailability near zero

phenylethylamine.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

garcinia cambogia extract 60% HCA.notes: Garcinia cambogia rind extract standardized to 60% hydroxycitric acid (HCA). HCA inhibits ATP-citrate lyase, theoretically reducing de novo lipogenesis. However, clinical evidence for weight loss is mixed to negative (Heymsfield 1998; Onakpoya 2011 meta-analysis). Modest effect at best.
aliases: super citrimax clinical strength garcinia cambogia extract
garcinia cambogia super citrimax clinical strength

garcinia cambogia extract 50% HCA.notes: Garcinia cambogia extract with 50% HCA standardization. Lower HCA concentration than 60% form. Clinical meta-analyses show only marginal weight loss benefit (~0.88 kg over placebo, Onakpoya 2011). FDA safety warning issued regarding liver injury reports. Efficacy-to-risk ratio is unfavorable.

garcinia cambogia (unspecified).notes: Garcinia gummi-gutta fruit rind; contains hydroxycitric acid (HCA). ATP citrate lyase inhibitor reducing lipogenesis. Meta-analyses show modest weight loss (~0.88 kg vs placebo). Hepatotoxicity reports. Form unspecified.

undecylenic_acid.description: C11 monounsaturated fatty acid derived from castor oil; FDA-approved OTC topical antifungal (21 CFR 333.210). Oral supplement form used for systemic Candida support.

10-undecenoic acid.notes: C11:1 fatty acid from castor oil pyrolysis. FDA OTC monograph ingredient for topical antifungal use (21 CFR 333.210). Oral bioavailability estimated 60-70% based on medium-chain fatty acid absorption kinetics. In vitro studies show fungistatic activity against Candida albicans at MIC 0.5-2 mM (PMID 11403866). Common oral dose 150-250 mg/d in divided doses.

sesame seed oil.notes: Sesamum indicum seed oil providing oleic and linoleic acids plus native sesame lignans. Human supplementation studies and recent reviews support cardiometabolic relevance, though outcomes vary by population and comparator oils (PMID 17004907; PMID 27055965). pumpkin seed oil.notes: Cucurbita pepo seed oil rich in linoleic acid, oleic acid, tocopherols, and phytosterols. Randomized human trials support symptom improvement in men with lower urinary tract symptoms / BPH using pumpkin seed oil or closely related Cucurbita pepo seed preparations (PMID 25252490; PMID 34666728).

extra_virgin_olive_oil.description: Extra-virgin olive oil used as an active dietary oil rather than a passive carrier

extra virgin olive oil.notes: Extra-virgin olive oil rich in oleic acid and olive polyphenols. Randomized trials and meta-analyses support cardiometabolic and oxidative-stress benefits when EVOO is intentionally consumed as a dietary oil intervention (PMID 34716791; PMID 37977313; PMID 40749711). Use this active oil route when the declared identity is olive oil itself, not an olive fruit polyphenol extract.

extra_virgin_olive_oil.cui_note: No confirmed exact UMLS concept found for extra virgin olive oil without collapsing to a broader olive oil term.
extra_virgin_olive_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

olive fruit extract standardized.notes: Olea europaea fruit extract standardized for olive polyphenols such as hydroxytyrosol. Fruit-derived olive phenolics support LDL oxidation protection and endothelial antioxidant status. Hytolive is a branded olive fruit extract standardized to hydroxytyrosol; the underlying identity is fruit-derived, not leaf-derived.

hydroxytyrosol (olive fruit phenolic).notes: Hydroxytyrosol-rich olive fruit phenolic fraction. Human crossover work showed rapid absorption and substantial metabolite recovery (PMID 19007325). EFSA recognizes olive oil polyphenols, including hydroxytyrosol and derivatives, for protection of LDL particles from oxidative damage. Use this fruit-specific route when the raw identity is olive fruit extract rather than olive leaf.
aliases: hytolive olive (olea europaea l.) extract hydroxytyrosol

absorption_structured.notes: Urinary recovery 35-55% as metabolites in human crossover; Tmax ~30 min; t1/2 ~1.5h (PMID 19007325, 21443285)

olive_fruit_extract.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup
olive_fruit_extract.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

coconut water powder.absorption: rapid oral hydration matrix; no special enhancement
coconut water powder.notes: Dehydrated coconut water / coconut liquid endosperm. Used in hydration and electrolyte formulas. Cococin is a branded coconut water ingredient. Human trials support coconut water as a reasonable oral rehydration ingredient in some exercise settings.

absorption_structured.notes: Hydration ingredient; no formal bioavailability fraction used

coconut water (unspecified).absorption: rapid oral hydration matrix; no special enhancement
coconut water (unspecified).notes: Coconut water ingredient without a specified powder or concentrate form. Conservative scoring.

coconut_water.notes: Potassium-rich coconut liquid endosperm used in hydration and electrolyte support products.

sphingomyelin (unspecified).notes: Sphingomyelin is a phosphocholine-containing sphingolipid found in milk fat globule membrane, egg, and marine phospholipid preparations. Added for exact identity coverage when the label discloses sphingomyelin directly.

absorption_structured.notes: moderate oral digestion to ceramide/sphingosine metabolites

beef_tallow.description: Rendered bovine fat used as a declared dietary fat ingredient when listed in the active section

beef tallow.notes: Rendered bovine adipose fat consisting mainly of oleic, palmitic, and stearic acids. This route is for labels that explicitly declare beef tallow itself as the active fat ingredient. Human supplement-specific efficacy data are lacking, so scoring is conservative and identity-focused.

absorption_structured.notes: dietary fat absorption is generally good

shuddha_laksha.description: Purified lac insect resin used in traditional Ayurvedic formulations

shuddha laksha.notes: Purified lac resin derived from the lac insect (Kerria lacca). Added as an exact identity route for Ayurvedic label rows where DSLD explicitly groups the ingredient under lac insect. Supplement-specific human evidence is limited, so this entry is identity-focused and conservatively scored.

shuddha_laksha.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

blubber oil.notes: Marine mammal blubber oil that naturally provides EPA, DHA, and docosapentaenoic acid (DPA). Used as a specialty omega-3 supplement in some legacy softgel products. Clinical literature often distinguishes seal oil from fish oil because of its comparatively higher DPA content.

seal_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for supplement ingredient
seal_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

milk basic protein (unspecified).notes: Milk basic protein (MBP) is a basic whey-protein fraction studied for bone health. Human and preclinical studies report support for bone formation markers and reduced bone resorption, so this is treated as a distinct dairy-derived active rather than generic whey protein.

milk_basic_protein.cui_note: No confirmed exact UMLS concept found for milk basic protein.

mead acid (20:3n-9).notes: Mead acid (20:3n-9) — the n-9 eicosatrienoic acid produced endogenously when essential fatty acids are deficient. Distinct from marine-derived ESA (20:3n-3). Conservative route for mead-acid label rows.

eicosatrienoic_acid.cui_note: No UMLS entry for supplement ingredient
eicosatrienoic_acid.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

pine nut oil (unspecified).notes: Pine nut oil used as a standalone oil supplement. Clinical interest is usually tied to its pinolenic-acid content, but this route preserves the oil identity without over-claiming a specific standardized composition.

pine_nut_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for supplement ingredient

krill_oil.notes: Omega-3 oil from Euphausia superba (Antarctic krill). EPA/DHA delivered in phospholipid form (~40%) rather than triglycerides, which may improve bioavailability (~33% higher AUC vs. fish oil TG, Schuchardt et al. 2011 PMC3168413). Contains natural astaxanthin (~0.1-0.2 mg/g) as antioxidant. Shellfish allergy warning required. Key brands: Neptune Krill Oil (NKO) and Superba Krill (Aker BioMarine, GRAS-notified).
krill_oil.cui_note: No UMLS entry for botanical/novel compound

standard krill oil.notes: Standard phospholipid-bound krill oil. EPA/DHA in phospholipid form (~40% phospholipids). ~33% higher AUC than standard fish oil triglycerides (Schuchardt et al. 2011, PMC3168413). Natural astaxanthin provides antioxidant protection against lipid oxidation. Shellfish allergen.

absorption_structured.notes: Phospholipid-bound omega-3s show ~33% higher AUC vs. fish oil TG (PMC3168413)

Neptune Krill Oil (NKO).notes: Neptune Krill Oil (NKO) — original branded krill oil with FDA NDI notification acceptance. Standardized phospholipid-bound omega-3s with astaxanthin. Manufactured by Neptune Technologies (Canada). Well-studied clinically for cardiovascular and PMS support.

Superba Krill (Aker BioMarine).notes: Superba Krill Oil by Aker BioMarine (Norway) — GRAS-notified, MSC-certified sustainable harvest. Superba2 is a concentrated upgrade. SuperbaBoost targets higher EPA/DHA density. ArcticPure is a retail brand using Superba.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Fatty Acid|Krill Oil | 534 (Number of products:191)

optiberry.notes: OptiBerry (originally InterHealth Nutraceuticals; now Lonza Group) is a standardized multi-berry anthocyanin blend combining wild blueberry (Vaccinium angustifolium), bilberry (V. myrtillus), cranberry (V. macrocarpon), elderberry (Sambucus nigra), raspberry seeds (Rubus idaeus), and strawberry (Fragaria × ananassa). ~60% anthocyanin phenolic content. In vitro/in vivo anti-angiogenic and antioxidant activity. 'OpriBerry' is a common OCR/label misread of OptiBerry.
optiberry.cui_note: No confirmed exact UMLS concept found for the proprietary OptiBerry blend.
optiberry.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for branded botanical blend

OptiBerry IH141.notes: OptiBerry IH141 — clinically studied formula. >50% tumor growth reduction in endothelioma model (PMID 14972022). Anthocyanin-rich with high ORAC. Preclinical anti-angiogenic evidence stronger than clinical RCT data. Standardized to ~60% total phenolics.

absorption_structured.notes: Anthocyanin absorption is low (<5% intact) but gut metabolites are bioactive

xylitol.absorption: Oral absorption ~50%, remainder fermented in colon
xylitol.notes: 5-carbon sugar alcohol with established anticariogenic activity. Inhibits Streptococcus mutans adhesion and acid production. Used therapeutically in dental health products at 1-10 g/day. GI tolerance threshold ~20 g/day.

absorption_structured.notes: Oral absorption ~50%, remainder fermented in colon

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|TBD|Xylitol | 2243 (Number of products:166)

guanidinoacetate.description: Guanidinoacetic acid (glycocyamine) — endogenous creatine precursor synthesized from arginine and glycine in the kidney. Supplemental GAA increases whole-body creatine stores; emerging sports-nutrition and clinical ingredient.

guanidinoacetate (unspecified).notes: Guanidinoacetic acid (GAA, glycocyamine), MW 117.1, CAS 352-97-6. Direct creatine precursor: GAA + SAMe → creatine via GAMT. Meta-analysis of 7 RCTs (PMID 31553717, Ostojic 2019) showed GAA at 1.2-3 g/day significantly increased muscle creatine in healthy adults. Subsequent RCT (PMID 34474639, Ostojic 2021) confirmed lean mass and anaerobic power improvement vs placebo at 3 g/day for 8 weeks. May deplete methyl donors (SAMe), so typically combined with betaine or methylation cofactors. Classified as amino acid derivative.

absorption_structured.notes: Small molecule (MW 117), amino acid derivative; high predicted oral absorption by BCS class analogy

guanidinoacetate.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

blood_orange_extract.description: Standardized extract of Sicilian blood orange (Citrus sinensis var. Moro) fruit and peel, rich in anthocyanins (cyanidin-3-glucoside). Morosil is the most studied branded form. Evidence for body composition support.

blood orange extract (unspecified).notes: Sicilian blood orange (Citrus sinensis var. Moro) extract standardized to anthocyanins. Morosil 400 mg/day RCT (n=60, 12 wks, PMID 26400477, Cardile 2015) reduced BMI, waist circumference, hip circumference vs placebo in overweight subjects. Anthocyanins (primarily cyanidin-3-glucoside, CAS 7084-24-4) activate AMPK and upregulate adiponectin in vitro. Also contains hydroxycinnamic acids and flavanones. Marketed as Morosil by Bionap.
aliases: sicilian blood orange fruit & peel extract
sicilian blood orange fruit and peel extract
sicilian blood orange fruit, peel extract
red orange fruit and peel extract
morosil sicilian red orange (citrus sinensis l. osbeck) (fruit juice) extract

absorption_structured.notes: Anthocyanin oral bioavailability is 1-5% (PMID 17177405); extensive phase II conjugation in gut and liver. Efficacy may depend on gut microbial metabolism.

blood_orange_extract.cui_note: No specific UMLS concept for blood orange extract
blood_orange_extract.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

oat_bran.description: Oat bran (Avena sativa) — outer layer of the oat groat, rich in soluble beta-glucan fiber. FDA-approved heart health claim at 3g/day. Distinct from oat straw (aerial parts).

oat bran (unspecified).absorption: fiber — not absorbed systemically; acts in gut lumen
oat bran (unspecified).notes: Oat bran contains 5-8% beta-glucan (soluble fiber). FDA health claim: 3g/day oat beta-glucan may reduce risk of coronary heart disease (21 CFR 101.81). Meta-analysis (PMID 25411276, Whitehead 2014) confirmed cholesterol-lowering effect. Mechanism: viscosity in small intestine reduces bile acid reabsorption, increases hepatic LDL receptor expression. Also supports glycemic control via delayed gastric emptying.

absorption_structured.notes: Soluble fiber — not systemically absorbed. Biological effect occurs in gut lumen via viscosity-dependent mechanism.

oat_bran.cui_note: UMLS C0795649 — oat bran (Organic Chemical, Pharmacologic Substance)

epigallocatechin.description: (-)-Epigallocatechin, minor catechin of green tea. C-3 epimer; <5% of total catechin fraction. Distinct from EGCG (epigallocatechin gallate).

epigallocatechin (unspecified).notes: (-)-Epigallocatechin (EGC), MW 306.3, CAS 970-74-1. Minor catechin of Camellia sinensis, typically <5% of total catechins. Low oral bioavailability shared with catechin class (<5%, PMID 29069445). Weaker antioxidant than EGCG. No standalone clinical trials; bioactivity derives from green tea mixtures. Scored conservatively.

absorption_structured.notes: Catechin class bioavailability <5% (PMID 29069445). Extensive phase II conjugation.

epigallocatechin.cui_note: UMLS C2348459 — Epigallocatechin (Organic Chemical). Distinct from EGCG C0059438.

l_aspartic_acid.description: L-Aspartic acid — standard proteinogenic amino acid. Distinct from D-aspartic acid (testosterone-related supplement). Used as mineral chelation carrier and in amino acid blends.

l-aspartic acid (unspecified).notes: L-Aspartic acid (L-Asp), MW 133.1, CAS 56-84-8. Standard proteinogenic amino acid, non-essential. Found in protein hydrolysates and amino acid blends. Distinct from D-aspartic acid (DAA, used for testosterone support). As a standalone supplement, limited clinical evidence — primarily functional as mineral chelation carrier (magnesium aspartate, zinc aspartate). PMID 6131674 (1984) showed aspartate salts improved fatigue resistance in animal models.

absorption_structured.notes: Standard amino acid; high oral absorption via intestinal amino acid transporters

l_aspartic_acid.cui_note: UMLS C0004015 — aspartic acid. Note: IQM d_aspartic_acid is the D-form used for testosterone; this is L-form.

yerba_mate.description: Ilex paraguariensis leaf extract — South American caffeinated herb. Contains caffeine, theobromine, chlorogenic acids, and saponins. Used for energy, weight management, antioxidant support.

yerba mate (unspecified).notes: Ilex paraguariensis leaf. Contains caffeine (1-2%), theobromine (0.3-0.5%), and chlorogenic acids. Systematic review (PMID 25732350, Gambero 2015) found evidence for anti-obesity effects via lipid metabolism modulation. RCT (PMID 25538068, Kim 2015, n=30) showed mate supplementation reduced body fat mass vs placebo. Caffeine component has high oral F (~99%); polyphenol component has low F (<5%). Traditional South American beverage.

absorption_structured.notes: Composite: caffeine ~99% F, polyphenols <5% F. Weighted estimate ~50% for bioactive fraction.

yerba_mate.cui_note: UMLS C1536546 — Yerba mate preparation (Organic Chemical, Pharmacologic Substance)

dill.description: Anethum graveolens — aromatic herb. Contains carvone, limonene, and apiol. Traditional digestive and antispasmodic use. Used in supplements for digestive support and women's health.

dill (unspecified).notes: Anethum graveolens herb/seed. Active compounds: carvone (up to 60% in seed oil), limonene, apiol. Traditional carminative and digestive herb. Preliminary evidence for lipid-lowering (PMID 24847471, Sahib 2014, n=150) and antispasmodic effects. Limited high-quality human RCT data for most claims. Used in GNC products as aerial parts extract and flower/leaf/stem preparations.

absorption_structured.notes: Volatile terpenoids (carvone, limonene) have moderate-good oral absorption; polyphenol fraction has lower F.

salacia.description: Salacia reticulata/chinensis root/stem extract — Ayurvedic herb for blood sugar management. Contains salacinol, kotalanol, and other alpha-glucosidase inhibitors.

salacia (unspecified).notes: Salacia reticulata/chinensis extract. Active compounds: salacinol (CAS 231588-02-4) and kotalanol — potent alpha-glucosidase inhibitors. Meta-analysis (PMID 29656413, Huang 2018) of 5 RCTs found significant reduction in postprandial glucose. Indian Ayurvedic herb (Saptrangi). MetaVive (OmniActive) is the main branded form with GRAS status.

absorption_structured.notes: Salacinol MW 334; moderate oral absorption estimated from structure-activity relationships

salacia.cui_note: UMLS C1337406 — salacia reticulata extract (Pharmacologic Substance)

touchi_extract.description: Fermented soybean (Glycine max) extract from Aspergillus oryzae fermentation. Contains alpha-glucosidase inhibitors. Japanese traditional food derivative for blood sugar management.

touchi extract (unspecified).notes: Touchi extract — water-soluble fraction of soybeans fermented with Aspergillus oryzae. Alpha-glucosidase inhibitor. RCT (PMID 15199382, Fujita 2004, n=60) showed 300 mg/meal significantly reduced postprandial glucose AUC vs placebo. Produced by Nippon Supplement Inc. (Japan). Has EFSA Novel Food evaluation. 300 mg/meal is typical dose.

absorption_structured.notes: Protein-derived alpha-glucosidase inhibitors; moderate oral absorption

touchi_extract.cui_note: No UMLS concept for touchi extract specifically

chitosan.description: Deacetylated chitin polymer from crustacean shells. Used as fat binder/weight management fiber. Also used in wound healing and drug delivery.

chitosan (unspecified).absorption: not systemically absorbed — acts in gut lumen
chitosan (unspecified).notes: Chitosan (poly-D-glucosamine), MW varies (50-1000 kDa). Derived from deacetylation of chitin from crustacean shells. Cochrane review (PMID 18254091, Jull 2008) found modest short-term weight loss effects (1.7 kg over placebo). Mechanism: binds dietary fat in gut lumen via electrostatic interaction, reducing absorption. Clinical dose: 1-3 g/day before meals. Allergen concern: shellfish-derived.

absorption_structured.notes: Polymer — not systemically absorbed. Acts as fat binder in GI tract.

chitosan.cui_note: UMLS C0162969 — chitosan (Organic Chemical, Pharmacologic Substance)

barley_grass.description: Young Hordeum vulgare grass/leaf. Rich in chlorophyll, superoxide dismutase (SOD), vitamins, and flavonoids. Used in greens supplements.

barley grass (unspecified).notes: Young barley grass (Hordeum vulgare) harvested before grain formation. Rich in chlorophyll, SOD, beta-carotene, and flavonoids. Review (PMID 29949890, Zeng 2018) summarized evidence for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and lipid-lowering properties. Limited high-quality human RCTs as standalone ingredient. Commonly found in greens blends and superfood powders.

absorption_structured.notes: Nutrient-dense plant matrix; fat-soluble vitamins have good F, water-soluble polyphenols variable

barley_grass.cui_note: No specific UMLS concept for barley grass supplement

calcium d-glucarate (unspecified).absorption: oral bioavailability not well-characterized; glucaric acid is the active metabolite
calcium d-glucarate (unspecified).notes: Calcium salt of D-glucaric acid. Inhibits beta-glucuronidase, supporting Phase II glucuronidation detoxification. Animal data (PMID:2023925, Walaszek 1990) shows inhibition of chemically induced tumors. Human RCT data limited. Widely used in integrative oncology support (Pure Encapsulations, Thorne). UNII SST07NLK7J, CAS 5793-88-4.

absorption_structured.notes: No definitive human oral bioavailability published

artemisinin (unspecified).absorption: low oral bioavailability (~32% in rats); lipophilic sesquiterpene lactone with rapid first-pass metabolism
artemisinin (unspecified).notes: Sesquiterpene lactone from Artemisia annua (Sweet Wormwood). Primarily known as antimalarial (Nobel Prize 2015, Tu Youyou). In supplement context, used for immune support and antimicrobial properties. Thorne's 'Artecin' uses standardized Artemisia annua extract. PMID:15264891 (Meshnick 2002) reviews mechanism. UNII 9RMU91N5K2, CAS 63968-64-9.

absorption_structured.notes: ~32% in rats (PMID:15264891); human F% likely similar or lower due to autoinduction of CYP2B6

isatis root extract.absorption: not well-characterized; active compounds include indirubin and tryptanthrin
isatis root extract.notes: Isatis tinctoria (Woad) root extract, known as Ban Lan Gen in TCM. Contains indirubin (anti-inflammatory, CYP inhibitor) and tryptanthrin (antimicrobial). Used traditionally for upper respiratory infections. Thorne's 'Isatis' product uses standardized root extract. PMID:19146932 (Heinemann 2008) reviews pharmacology. UNII T538276W1L.

absorption_structured.notes: No definitive human PK data for crude extract

isatis.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for Isatis tinctoria plant extract

7-keto dhea (unspecified).absorption: oral bioavailability not definitively established; well-absorbed based on serum level studies
7-keto dhea (unspecified).notes: 7-Keto-DHEA (7-oxodehydroepiandrosterone) is a non-hormonal metabolite of DHEA that does NOT convert to testosterone or estrogen. Distinct from DHEA (CUI C0011185, UNII 459AG36T1B). Supports thermogenesis via increased thyroid hormone activity. DB-RCT (PMID:17227968, Zenk 2007, n=30, 7 wk): 7-Keto 200mg/day + exercise lost 2.88 kg vs 0.97 kg placebo. Also PMID:10435590 (Lardy 1998) on thermogenic mechanism. UNII 2334LJD2E9, CAS 566-19-8.

absorption_structured.notes: Well-absorbed orally; definitive F% not published

cascara sagrada bark extract.absorption: Anthraquinone glycosides (cascarosides) hydrolyzed by gut bacteria to active aglycones
cascara sagrada bark extract.notes: Cascara Sagrada (Frangula purshiana bark). Contains cascarosides A-D (anthraquinone glycosides). Stimulant laxative acting on colonic smooth muscle. FDA reclassified as Category III (insufficient data) in 2002 but still legal in supplements. UNII 4VBP01X99F, CAS 8015-89-2.

blue flag root extract.notes: Iris versicolor (Blue Flag) root. Traditional use as cholagogue and mild laxative. Contains iridin and other isoflavones. Limited modern clinical evidence. UNII X43D4L3DQC.

chinese rhubarb root extract.absorption: Anthraquinones (emodin, rhein) well-absorbed; sennosides require colonic activation
chinese rhubarb root extract.notes: Rheum palmatum (Chinese Rhubarb / Da Huang). Contains anthraquinones (emodin, rhein, chrysophanol) and sennosides. Used in TCM for constipation, inflammation, and as cholagogue. Modern evidence for mild laxative effect. UNII G025DAL7CE.

absorption_structured.notes: Anthraquinones well-absorbed; sennoside prodrugs activated by gut flora

chinese_rhubarb.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept found via GSRS lookup

frangula.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for Frangula alnus botanical preparation

frangula bark extract.absorption: Anthraquinone glycosides (glucofrangulin) hydrolyzed by colonic bacteria to active frangulin/emodin
frangula bark extract.notes: Frangula alnus (Alder Buckthorn) bark. Contains glucofrangulin A/B (anthraquinone glycosides). Stimulant laxative similar to cascara/senna. European Pharmacopoeia monograph requires 1-year aging of bark. UNII 824G7B958Y.

absorption_structured.notes: Prodrug; glycosides activated by colonic bacteria

glutamate.description: Non-essential amino acid; excitatory neurotransmitter precursor. Supplemented as L-glutamic acid or MSG.

l-glutamate.notes: Abundant amino acid absorbed efficiently in the gut. Supplemental glutamate primarily supports gut mucosal health and as a flavor enhancer. Most dietary intake is from protein-rich foods.

absorption_structured.notes: Rapidly absorbed via active transport in small intestine

glutamate.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

oleic_acid.description: Omega-9 monounsaturated fatty acid abundant in olive oil and avocado. Supports cardiovascular health.

oleic acid.notes: Primary MUFA in olive oil and Mediterranean diet. Well-absorbed as a triglyceride. FDA qualified health claim for oleic acid and reduced coronary heart disease risk.

absorption_structured.notes: Lipase-mediated hydrolysis and efficient absorption via lymphatic system

oleic_acid.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

citric_acid.description: Organic acid used as a mineral chelation enhancer and acidulant in supplements. Found naturally in citrus fruits.

citric acid.notes: Ubiquitous organic acid primarily used as excipient/acidulant. Enhances mineral absorption (calcium citrate, magnesium citrate). Rapidly absorbed and metabolized via Krebs cycle.

absorption_structured.notes: Completely absorbed; enters Krebs cycle directly

citric_acid.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

kaempferol.description: Dietary flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective properties. Found in broccoli, tea, and berries.

kaempferol.notes: Poorly bioavailable flavonoid; extensively metabolized first-pass. Research shows anti-inflammatory effects via NF-κB inhibition and antioxidant activity. Typical supplemental dose 100-500 mg.

absorption_structured.notes: Extensive first-pass metabolism; glucuronidation limits systemic availability

kaempferol.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

nad.description: Essential coenzyme in cellular energy metabolism and DNA repair. Supplemented via precursors NMN and NR (nicotinamide riboside).

nad+.notes: Supplemented primarily via precursors NMN (nicotinamide mononucleotide) and NR (nicotinamide riboside) which boost intracellular NAD+ levels. Direct oral NAD+ has limited bioavailability. Typical NMN dose 250-1000 mg/day.

absorption_structured.notes: NR/NMN precursors have better oral bioavailability than direct NAD+; converted intracellularly

nad.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

naringenin.description: Citrus flavanone with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Found in grapefruit and citrus peel.

naringenin.notes: Grapefruit-derived flavanone; potent CYP3A4 inhibitor (grapefruit effect). Antioxidant and hepatoprotective in preclinical studies. Caution: may alter drug metabolism.

absorption_structured.notes: Heavily glucuronidated; CYP3A4 inhibition is a pharmacological concern

naringenin.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

vanadium.description: Trace element with insulin-mimetic properties. Supplemented as vanadyl sulfate. No established RDA; UMLS classifies as potentially hazardous at supplemental doses.

vanadyl sulfate.notes: Insulin-mimetic trace mineral marketed for blood sugar support. Poor oral bioavailability (~1-5%). FDA has not established an RDA. UMLS flags as 'Hazardous or Poisonous Substance'. Doses >1.8 mg/day associated with GI toxicity. Use with caution.

absorption_structured.notes: Very poor absorption; mainly excreted renally; accumulates in bone

vanadium.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

soybean.description: Source plant for soy protein, isoflavones, and lecithin supplements. Rich in phytoestrogens.

soybean extract.notes: Soybean products include soy protein isolate, soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein), soy lecithin, and soy fiber. Phytoestrogenic activity from isoflavones. Common allergen (Big 8).

absorption_structured.notes: Isoflavone absorption varies; soy protein well digested (PDCAAS 0.91-1.0)

soybean.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

sunflower_oil.description: Vegetable oil rich in linoleic acid (omega-6) and vitamin E. Used as a carrier oil in softgel supplements.

sunflower oil.notes: Primarily used as excipient/carrier oil in vitamin D, E, and omega-3 softgels. High in linoleic acid (~65%) and alpha-tocopherol. Generally well tolerated.

absorption_structured.notes: Efficiently absorbed as triglycerides via lipase hydrolysis

sunflower_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

silymarin.description: Active flavonolignan complex from milk thistle (Silybum marianum). Hepatoprotective and antioxidant.

silymarin complex.notes: Standardized milk thistle extract (70-80% silymarin). Primary active: silybin. Hepatoprotective via antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-fibrotic mechanisms. Typical dose 140-420 mg/day. Phytosome formulation improves bioavailability 4-10x.

silymarin.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

oligosaccharides.description: Short-chain carbohydrates (FOS, GOS, XOS) that serve as prebiotic substrates for beneficial gut bacteria.

oligosaccharides.notes: Fructo-oligosaccharides (FOS), galacto-oligosaccharides (GOS), and xylo-oligosaccharides (XOS) resist digestion and ferment in the colon, promoting Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus growth. Typical dose 2-10 g/day.

absorption_structured.notes: Designed to resist digestion; fermented by colonic microbiota

oligosaccharides.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

canola_oil.description: Rapeseed-derived oil rich in oleic acid and ALA. Used as carrier oil in softgel supplements.

canola oil.notes: Low erucic acid rapeseed oil. Contains ~7% ALA (omega-3), ~61% oleic acid (omega-9). Used as excipient in vitamin softgels. UMLS preferred name is Brassica napus (botanical source).

canola_oil.cui_note: UMLS preferred name is Brassica napus (source plant); used here for canola oil ingredient
canola_oil.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

orotic_acid.description: Pyrimidine precursor used as a mineral carrier (magnesium orotate, lithium orotate). Historically called vitamin B13 but not officially recognized as a vitamin.

orotic acid.notes: Used primarily as a chelation form for minerals (orotates). Magnesium orotate and lithium orotate are popular supplement forms. The B13 designation is not officially recognized. Well absorbed orally.

absorption_structured.notes: Orally well absorbed; rapidly converted in pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway

orotic_acid.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

rice_bran.description: Outer layer of rice grain rich in gamma-oryzanol, tocotrienols, and phytic acid (IP6). Used as a fiber and antioxidant supplement.

rice bran extract.notes: Contains gamma-oryzanol (ferulic acid esters), tocotrienols, and IP6. Gamma-oryzanol is the primary active, marketed for cholesterol support. Typical dose 100-500 mg/day.

absorption_structured.notes: Gamma-oryzanol absorption ~10%; oil-based forms improve it

rice_bran.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

palmitoleic_acid.description: Omega-7 monounsaturated fatty acid found in sea buckthorn and macadamia nut oil. Supports metabolic and skin health.

palmitoleic acid.notes: Omega-7 fatty acid gaining attention for metabolic health. Found in sea buckthorn berry oil and macadamia nut oil. Typical dose 210-420 mg/day. Purified omega-7 supplements available.

absorption_structured.notes: Well absorbed as triglyceride via standard lipid absorption pathway

palmitoleic_acid.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

idebenone.description: Synthetic short-chain analog of CoQ10 with antioxidant and neuroprotective properties. Better blood-brain barrier penetration than CoQ10.

idebenone.notes: Synthetic benzoquinone structurally related to CoQ10 but with shorter side chain. Crosses BBB more effectively. Used as supplement for cognitive support and mitochondrial function. Also a prescription drug in some countries (Raxone/Catena). Typical dose 60-300 mg/day.

absorption_structured.notes: Better BBB penetration than CoQ10; absorption improved with fat

idebenone.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient

lecithin.description: Phospholipid mixture (primarily phosphatidylcholine) from soy or sunflower. Supports brain health and liver function.

lecithin.notes: Primary phospholipid source providing phosphatidylcholine (PC), which is a choline source. Soy lecithin and sunflower lecithin are the two main forms. Typical dose 1200-2400 mg/day. Important for cell membrane integrity and acetylcholine synthesis.

absorption_structured.notes: Phospholipids well absorbed via pancreatic phospholipase A2; PC converted to choline and glycerophosphocholine

lecithin.rxcui_note: No RxNorm concept for supplement ingredient
